content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/tomblume/.claude/plugins/cache/beads-marketplace/beads/0.46.0/skills/beads

# Beads - Persistent Task Memory for AI Agents

Graph-based issue tracker that survives conversation compaction. Provides persistent memory for multi-session work with complex dependencies.

## bd vs TodoWrite

| bd (persistent) | TodoWrite (ephemeral) |
|-----------------|----------------------|
| Multi-session work | Single-session tasks |
| Complex dependencies | Linear execution |
| Survives compaction | Conversation-scoped |
| Git-backed, team sync | Local to session |

**Decision test**: "Will I need this context in 2 weeks?" → YES = bd

**When to use bd**:
- Work spans multiple sessions or days
- Tasks have dependencies or blockers
- Need to survive conversation compaction
- Exploratory/research work with fuzzy boundaries
- Collaboration with team (git sync)

**When to use TodoWrite**:
- Single-session linear tasks
- Simple checklist for immediate work
- All context is in current conversation
- Will complete within current session

## Prerequisites

```bash
bd --version  # Requires v0.34.0+
```

- **bd CLI** installed and in PATH
- **Git repository** (bd requires git for sync)
- **Initialization**: `bd init` run once (humans do this, not agents)

## CLI Reference

**Run `bd prime`** for AI-optimized workflow context (auto-loaded by hooks).
**Run `bd <command> --help`** for specific command usage.

Essential commands: `bd ready`, `bd create`, `bd show`, `bd update`, `bd close`, `bd sync`

## Session Protocol

1. `bd ready` — Find unblocked work
2. `bd show <id>` — Get full context
3. `bd update <id> --status in_progress` — Start work
4. Add notes as you work (critical for compaction survival)
5. `bd close <id> --reason "..."` — Complete task
6. `bd sync` — Persist to git (always run at session end)

## Advanced Features

| Feature | CLI | Resource |
|---------|-----|----------|
| Molecules (templates) | `bd mol --help` | [MOLECULES.md](resources/MOLECULES.md) |
| Chemistry (pour/wisp) | `bd pour`, `bd wisp` | [CHEMISTRY_PATTERNS.md](resources/CHEMISTRY_PATTERNS.md) |
| Agent beads | `bd agent --help` | [AGENTS.md](resources/AGENTS.md) |
| Async gates | `bd gate --help` | [ASYNC_GATES.md](resources/ASYNC_GATES.md) |
| Worktrees | `bd worktree --help` | [WORKTREES.md](resources/WORKTREES.md) |

## Resources

| Resource | Content |
|----------|---------|
| [BOUNDARIES.md](resources/BOUNDARIES.md) | bd vs TodoWrite detailed comparison |
| [CLI_REFERENCE.md](resources/CLI_REFERENCE.md) | Complete command syntax |
| [DEPENDENCIES.md](resources/DEPENDENCIES.md) | Dependency system deep dive |
| [INTEGRATION_PATTERNS.md](resources/INTEGRATION_PATTERNS.md) | TodoWrite and tool integration |
| [ISSUE_CREATION.md](resources/ISSUE_CREATION.md) | When and how to create issues |
| [MOLECULES.md](resources/MOLECULES.md) | Proto definitions, component labels |
| [PATTERNS.md](resources/PATTERNS.md) | Common usage patterns |
| [RESUMABILITY.md](resources/RESUMABILITY.md) | Compaction survival guide |
| [STATIC_DATA.md](resources/STATIC_DATA.md) | Database schema reference |
| [TROUBLESHOOTING.md](resources/TROUBLESHOOTING.md) | Error handling and fixes |
| [WORKFLOWS.md](resources/WORKFLOWS.md) | Step-by-step workflow patterns |
| [AGENTS.md](resources/AGENTS.md) | Agent bead tracking (v0.40+) |
| [ASYNC_GATES.md](resources/ASYNC_GATES.md) | Human-in-the-loop gates |
| [CHEMISTRY_PATTERNS.md](resources/CHEMISTRY_PATTERNS.md) | Mol vs Wisp decision tree |
| [WORKTREES.md](resources/WORKTREES.md) | Parallel development patterns |

## Full Documentation

- **bd prime**: AI-optimized workflow context
- **GitHub**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads)

input.description: Run bd prime to get workflow context

content.content:
# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd sync                 (commit beads changes)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 5. bd sync                 (commit any new beads changes)
[ ] 6. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: hooks auto-sync, run `bd sync` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="..." --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --status=in_progress` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd sync` - Sync with git remote (run at session end)
- `bd sync --status` - Check sync status without syncing

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --status=in_progress  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
bd sync                     # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

content.text:
Context recovered. Here's the session state for the `share-platform` repo:

**Platform-level board: clean slate**
- 26 issues total, all **closed** — no open work here
- The module-specific work (share-ui, sharemember-app, etc.) tracks its own issues

The platform repo is fully caught up from the last session. The recent activity (5 issues created, 1 closed in last 24h) matches the QR vouching feature work that was completed (commit `061de43`).

**Next steps options:**
- Check module repos for outstanding work: `bd ready` in `../share-ui/`, `../operator-ts/`, etc.
- Look at the untracked file from git status: `docs/trash-bin-feature-implementation.md` — this may need to be committed or reviewed
- Start new cross-cutting work with `bd create`

The `docs/trash-bin-feature-implementation.md` file caught my eye from the git status. Want me to look at that, or are you starting something new?

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This plan uses **temporal relationships** in Neo4j — instead of deleting nodes, it timestamps the edges. This is a well-established graph database pattern. Because Neo4j allows multiple relationships of the same type between two nodes, you can have both an old `BELONGS_TO {validUntil: "2026-03-02"}` and a restored `BELONGS_TO {validUntil: NULL}` on the same document simultaneously — something that would require a nullable column + extra logic in a relational DB.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

This is a solid, self-contained implementation plan for a Trash Bin feature. Here's a summary:

**What it does:**
- Soft-delete documents by setting `validUntil` on `BELONGS_TO` / `SHARED_WITH` / `CONTRIBUTED_TO` relationships
- Mirrors the existing `SeatRelationship.start/end` pattern already used in roundtables
- Permanent delete also cleans up MinIO (S3) files — no orphaned blobs

**Scope:**
| Layer | Files | Work |
|---|---|---|
| Backend | `documents.repository.ts`, `.service.ts`, `.controller.ts` | 4 new methods each |
| Frontend | `DocumentsView.tsx` + new `TrashView.tsx` | New tab + component |
| Migration | Cypher script | Backfill `validFrom`/`validUntil` on existing rels |

**One thing worth noting:** The `getTrashedDocuments` Cypher query (line 108-119) has a subtle issue — the `WHERE creator.id = $shareholderId` clause is inside the `OPTIONAL MATCH`, not after the outer `MATCH`, which means it would filter the `OPTIONAL MATCH` joins but not restrict which documents are returned. It would show *all* trashed documents, not just those owned/modified by this shareholder. That's probably a bug in the plan that needs fixing before implementation.

Do you want to implement this? I can create beads issues and start work.

content.thinking:
Now I have a good understanding of the QR code vouch feature. Let me understand the architecture:

1. **VerifyPage** - The page where un-identified users can get verified. It now has two paths:
   - **eID path** - the primary identification method
   - **Peer vouching path** - an alternative with two modes:
     - **QR mode** - creates an "open" vouch request, shows QR code, polls for confirmation
     - **Email mode** - sends a vouch request to a specific email

2. **QR flow**:
   - `POST /v2/sharemember/vouch-requests/open` → creates an open vouch request, returns `{id}`
   - The QR code encodes a link to the vouch page with the request ID, then continuously checks the request status every 3 seconds until it's confirmed, at which point it refreshes the user session and redirects to the profile page

3. **VouchPage** handles specific error cases:
   - 403 when the voucher themselves isn't identified
   - 400 when someone tries to vouch for themselves

I'm realizing the user is probably sharing this as context for what they want to do next—maybe they want me to document this in the memory file, update the docs, or commit the changes. Let me ask what they'd like to do with this feature.

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
The QR flow uses **polling** (`setInterval` every 3s) instead of WebSockets for status updates. This is pragmatic for a low-frequency event like a human scanning a QR code — no need for a persistent socket connection. The `pollRef` with cleanup in `useEffect` return prevents the interval from leaking when the component unmounts.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Got the full picture. Here's what was built:

**QR Vouch Flow (`VerifyPage`):**
1. `POST /v2/sharemember/vouch-requests/open` → creates an "open" (unclaimed) vouch request
2. QR code encodes `${origin}/vouch/${requestId}` — the voucher scans and lands on `VouchPage`
3. Polls `GET /v2/sharemember/vouch-requests/${requestId}/status` every 3s
4. On `confirmed` → refreshes user token, redirects to `/profile`

**VouchModeChooser** splits the existing email flow and new QR flow — QR is the primary CTA, email is the secondary link.

**VouchPage** now handles the two new error states from the open-request auto-claim: `403` (voucher not identified) and `400` (self-vouch attempt).

---

- **Commit the untracked `docs/trash-bin-feature-implementation.md`** 
- Something else (backend changes still needed? Deploy?)

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/tomblume/.claude/skills/devops

# shaRe Platform DevOps

Manage the shaRe platform on the Scaleway production server.

## Server

- **IP**: `51.15.211.196`
- **SSH**: `ssh -i ~/.ssh/scaleway_ed25519 root@51.15.211.196`
- **Infra dir on server**: `/opt/share-platform/infra`
- **Compose files**: `docker-compose.prod.yml`, `docker-compose.dev.yml`
- **Env files**: `.env.prod`, `.env.dev`

## Service Map

| Service | Image | Compose service name | Source repo |
|---------|-------|---------------------|-------------|
| operator | `ghcr.io/share-community/operator:latest` | `operator-{env}` | `../operator-ts/` |
| share-ui | `ghcr.io/share-community/share-ui:latest` | `share-ui-{env}` | `../share-ui/` (Dockerfile in `../u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/share-ui/Dockerfile`) |
| sharemember-app | `ghcr.io/share-community/sharemember-app:latest` | `sharemember-app-{env}` | `../sharemember-app/` (Dockerfile in `../u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/sharemember-app/Dockerfile`) — **prod only** |
| keycloak | `ghcr.io/share-community/sharemembers:latest` | `keycloak-{env}` | `../sharemembers/` |
| neo4j | (standard image) | `neo4j-{env}` | — |
| redis | (standard image) | `redis-{env}` | — |
| postgres | `postgres:16-alpine` | `postgres-{env}` | — |
| odoo | `ghcr.io/share-community/odoo:latest` | `odoo-{env}` | `../u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/odoo/` |
| onlyoffice | `onlyoffice/documentserver:latest` | `onlyoffice-{env}` | — |
| drawio | `jgraph/drawio:latest` | `drawio-{env}` | — |
| minio | `minio/minio:latest` | `minio-{env}` | — |
| metabase | `metabase/metabase:latest` | `metabase-{env}` | — |
| traefik | — | `coolify-proxy` (shared) | — |

Container naming: `share-{env}-{service}-{env}-1` (e.g. `share-prod-operator-prod-1`)

### Postgres — shared instance, multiple databases

| Database | Owner | Used by |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `keycloak` | keycloak | Keycloak auth |
| `odoodb` | odoo | Odoo 18 |
| `sharedb` | share | operator-ts analytics (future) |
| `metabase` | metabase | Metabase analytics |

### Odoo — self-hosted Odoo 18 Community

- **URL**: `https://odoo.wurzelwerk.up2go.com`
- **DB**: `odoodb` on shared `postgres-prod`
- **Admin user**: `admin` (password in `.env.prod` as `ODOO_ADMIN_PASSWD`)
- **Service user** (used by operator-ts): `operator@share.local` (password = `ODOO_API_KEY` in `.env.prod`)
- **Installed modules**: `mail` (includes discuss.channel), `calendar`, `project`, `account`
- **Healthcheck**: `https://odoo.wurzelwerk.up2go.com/web/health` (expect `{"status":"pass"}`)

### Metabase — analytics & reporting

- **URL**: `https://metabase.up2go.com` (platform-admin only, basic auth protected)
- **DB**: `metabase` on shared `postgres-prod`
- **Data sources**: connect to `sharedb` (postgresql://postgres-prod:5432/sharedb) for analytics
- **Healthcheck**: `https://metabase.up2go.com/api/health` (expect 200)

## Commands

Parse `` to determine the action. Default env is `prod`.

### `deploy <service> [env]`

Build, transfer, and restart a service:

1. **Build** the Docker image from the source repo
2. **Transfer** via `docker save | ssh ... docker load`
3. **Restart** via `docker compose up -d <service-env>`
4. **ALWAYS restart Traefik** after any container recreate: `docker restart coolify-proxy`
5. **Wait 5 seconds**, then verify the service started by checking logs
6. **Verify all routes** respond (200/302)

Build commands per service:
```bash
# operator
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/operator-ts
docker build -t ghcr.io/share-community/operator:latest .

# share-ui
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/share-ui
docker build -t ghcr.io/share-community/share-ui:latest \
  -f /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/share-ui/Dockerfile .

# sharemember-app
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app
docker build -t ghcr.io/share-community/sharemember-app:latest \
  -f /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/sharemember-app/Dockerfile .

# keycloak
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemembers
docker build -t ghcr.io/share-community/sharemembers:latest .

# odoo (custom image with OCA extra-addons support)
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/u2g-infrastructure-ts/services/odoo
docker build -t ghcr.io/share-community/odoo:latest .
```

Restart on server:
```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/scaleway_ed25519 root@51.15.211.196 \
  "cd /opt/share-platform/infra && \
   docker compose -f docker-compose.{env}.yml -p share-{env} --env-file .env.{env} \
   up -d {service-name}"
```

### `status [env]`

Check all container status and route health:
```bash
SSH="ssh -i ~/.ssh/scaleway_ed25519 root@51.15.211.196"
$SSH "docker ps --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}' | grep share-{env}"
```

Then check routes:
- `https://wurzelwerk.up2go.com` (share-ui, expect 200)
- `https://id.wurzelwerk.up2go.com` (sharemember-app, expect 200 — prod only)
- `https://sso.wurzelwerk.up2go.com` (keycloak, expect 302)
- `https://operator.wurzelwerk.up2go.com/health` (operator direct, expect 200)
- `https://wurzelwerk.up2go.com/api/health` (operator via api, expect 200)
- `https://odoo.wurzelwerk.up2go.com/web/health` (odoo, expect 200 with `{"status":"pass"}`)
- `https://metabase.up2go.com/api/health` (metabase, expect 200 — basic auth required)

Dev routes use `share.dev.up2go.com`, `sso.dev.sharemembers.net`, `operator.dev.up2go.com`.

### `restart <service|traefik> [env]`

Restart a specific service or Traefik:
- **traefik**: `docker restart coolify-proxy` (fixes 504/CORS issues)
- **other**: `docker compose ... restart {service-env}`

### `logs <service> [env] [lines]`

Show recent logs (default 30 lines):
```bash
ssh ... "docker logs share-{env}-{service}-{env}-1 --tail {lines}"
```

### `neo4j <cypher> [env]`

Run a Cypher query against Neo4j:
```bash
ssh ... 'docker exec share-{env}-neo4j-{env}-1 cypher-shell \
  -u neo4j \
  -p $(docker inspect share-{env}-neo4j-{env}-1 \
    --format "{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}" | grep NEO4J_AUTH | cut -d/ -f2) \
  "<cypher query>"'
```

### `diagnose`

Run full diagnostic:
1. Check all container status
2. Check all route health codes
3. Check for errors in operator logs (last 50 lines)
4. Check memory usage on server
5. Report findings and suggest fixes

## Known Issues

- **504 Gateway Timeout after deploy**: Traefik loses routing to recreated containers. Fix: `docker restart coolify-proxy`
- **CORS errors from sso.wurzelwerk...**: Same cause as above — Keycloak is unreachable via Traefik
- **Keycloak startup takes ~4 minutes**: Wait for `healthy` status before testing auth flows

## Important

- Always restart Traefik (`coolify-proxy`) after deploying any service
- After deploy, verify ALL routes (not just the deployed service)
- Never expose Neo4j credentials in output — use the `docker inspect` pattern
- Default to `prod` environment unless explicitly told otherwise

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Adding a dedicated `vouch_back_to_qr` key (rather than reusing `vouch_back`) keeps the two contexts semantically separate — the QR mode's "back" means "cancel and go back to the chooser", while this one means "switch to a different mode". Separate keys also make future copy changes easier without risk of accidentally affecting the other button.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Deployed and pushed. Der Button in der E-Mail-Ansicht zeigt jetzt **"Stattdessen QR-Code anzeigen"** (DE) bzw. "Show QR code instead" (EN). Der "Zurück"-Button im QR-Modus ist unverändert.

message.content: wenn ich den button "Show QR code" betätige, dann erscheint noch ein "Show QR code" button, anstatt der QR Code. Warum ist das so?

content.thinking:
The user is reporting a bug: when they click "Show QR code" button, another "Show QR code" button appears instead of the actual QR code. Let me re-read the `QrVouchMode` component to understand why.

Looking at the `QrVouchMode` component code I read earlier:

```typescript
function QrVouchMode({ onBack }: { onBack: () => void }): JSX.Element {
  // ...
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<'idle' | 'loading' | 'showing' | 'confirmed' | 'error'>('idle')


```

I see the issue now. The `handleShowQr` function sets the status to 'loading' and then 'showing' after creating the voucher request, and there's a conditional that should render the QR code when status is 'showing' and requestId exists. But the return statement at the end is being cut off—it looks like the button is being rendered in the default case instead of the QR code being shown when it should be.

The real problem is that the endpoint returning 404 means the request creation is failing, so we never actually get to the 'showing' state where the QR code would display. I need to check why that endpoint is returning 404 and fix the API call. Looking at the render logic, the button always displays in the default state regardless of status — so if the request fails silently without setting an error state, the user would just see the "Show QR code" button reappear without any error message to indicate what went wrong.

The real culprit is probably that the API call succeeds with a 201 response, but `req.id` is null or undefined, so `setRequestId(req.id)` leaves requestId falsy and the condition `status === 'showing' && requestId` never evaluates to true, falling back to the default render instead of showing the QR code.

Alternatively, the API might still be returning 404 (Traefik could be caching the old routing), triggering the catch block and setting status to 'error', which would display the "Show QR code" button again with an error message below it—matching what the user is seeing.

Let me check the operator logs to see if the `/open` endpoint is actually working now, or if the user tested this before the deploy fully propagated.

Defining the API function... The user mentioned they're testing this right now after the deploy, so the endpoint should be working. Let me check the operator logs to see what's actually happening when they click the button.

content.thinking:
I can see the `@Post('open')` endpoint is in the controller at line 45. But it's not showing up in the Route log. This is a NestJS routing issue.

The problem is likely **route ordering**. In NestJS, `@Post('open')` and `@Post()` are defined, but then there are also `@Get(':requestId/status')`, `@Get(':requestId')`, and `@Post(':requestId/confirm')`, `@Post(':requestId/decline')`.

The issue is that `@Post('open')` is defined AFTER `@Post()`, and `@Get(':requestId')` with a path parameter `:requestId` would match `/open` as a parameter value. But for POST requests, the parameter routes might be conflicting...


Actually, looking at the startup log more carefully, I'm counting the registered routes and comparing them against what's defined in the controller. The log shows five routes total, but I need to account for all the decorators I'm seeing in the code. I'm noticing a discrepancy—I listed 7 routes but only 5 are actually showing up in the current logs, with the POST endpoints for creating and opening vouch requests missing. Looking back at the old operator logs, there was already an error trying to POST to the open endpoint, so something's been broken across deploys.

But wait, I'm realizing the startup logs I'm looking at only capture the last 20 lines, which means the route registration output happened earlier and scrolled off. The routes are definitely registered in the controller code, I just didn't capture that part of the output. Let me trace through what's actually visible in the logs more carefully.

I can see the vouch request endpoints listed there, which confirms the routing is working. So if the routes are fine, the real problem must be elsewhere. The user mentioned clicking "Show QR code" displays another "Show QR code" button instead of the QR code itself. Let me look at the QrVouchMode component logic to understand what's happening with the state transitions.

The component starts in idle state showing the button, then on click it sets loading state. When the request succeeds, it should set the requestId and switch to showing state to render the QR code. But if the user keeps seeing the button, either the request is failing silently and hitting the error state, or the request succeeds but requestId remains null, leaving it in showing state without the actual QR code to display. There's also something about the VouchSection component switching between choose and qr modes that might be relevant here. When `QrVouchMode` first renders with `status = 'idle'`, it displays the same "QR-Code anzeigen" button again, forcing the user to click it a second time before the API call actually triggers. That's the real bug—the component should either skip the idle state button or immediately fetch the QR code on mount. So the cleanest approach is to add a `useEffect` hook that fires on component mount and immediately calls `handleShowQr()`, eliminating the extra click and getting the QR code displayed right away.

content.text:
Ich sehe das Problem. `QrVouchMode` startet im `'idle'`-Zustand und zeigt dann nochmal denselben "QR-Code anzeigen" Button — der Nutzer muss **zweimal** klicken. Der erste Klick (im `VouchModeChooser`) wechselt nur den Modus, der zweite Klick im `QrVouchMode` triggert erst die API.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Dieses "Doppelklick"-Problem entsteht, weil der VouchModeChooser nur den UI-Zustand wechselt (`setMode('qr')`), aber `QrVouchMode` dann nochmal wartet. Fix: `handleShowQr()` automatisch beim Mounten via `useEffect` aufrufen — kein zweiter Klick nötig.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

content.thinking:
The default render (for `idle` and `loading` and `error` states) shows:
1. A button to show QR (or "starting..." when loading)
2. Optional error message
3. A back button

Since we now auto-trigger on mount, the `idle` state will be very brief (immediately transitions to `loading`). 

But for the `error` state, we still want the button (so the user can retry). And for `loading`, we want some feedback. So the existing code is actually fine as-is:
- `idle` → briefly shows button (then immediately auto-calls handleShowQr)
- `loading` → shows disabled button with "starting..." text


- `error` → shows button again with the error message so the user can try again

The fallback render handles all the necessary feedback and retry logic, so the main fix was just adding the auto-trigger to the useEffect. Ready to build and deploy.

content.content: Launching skill: beads:list

content.text:
List beads issues with optional filtering.

## Basic Filters

- **--status, -s**: Filter by status (open, in_progress, blocked, closed)
- **--priority, -p**: Filter by priority (0-4: 0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low, 4=backlog)
- **--type, -t**: Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
- **--assignee, -a**: Filter by assignee
- **--label, -l**: Filter by labels (comma-separated, must have ALL labels)
- **--label-any**: Filter by labels (OR semantics, must have AT LEAST ONE)
- **--title**: Filter by title text (case-insensitive substring match)
- **--limit, -n**: Limit number of results

## Advanced Filters

### Pattern Matching
- **--title-contains**: Search for text in title (case-insensitive)
- **--desc-contains**: Search for text in description (case-insensitive)
- **--notes-contains**: Search for text in notes (case-insensitive)

### Date Ranges
- **--created-after**: Issues created after date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601)
- **--created-before**: Issues created before date
- **--updated-after**: Issues updated after date
- **--updated-before**: Issues updated before date
- **--closed-after**: Issues closed after date
- **--closed-before**: Issues closed before date

### Priority Range
- **--priority-min**: Minimum priority (inclusive)
- **--priority-max**: Maximum priority (inclusive)

### Empty/Null Checks
- **--empty-description**: Find issues with no description
- **--no-assignee**: Find unassigned issues
- **--no-labels**: Find issues with no labels

## Examples

### Basic Usage
- `bd list --status open --priority 1`: High priority open issues
- `bd list --type bug --assignee alice`: Alice's assigned bugs
- `bd list --label backend,needs-review`: Backend issues needing review
- `bd list --title "auth"`: Issues with "auth" in the title

### Advanced Usage
- `bd list --title-contains "auth" --status open`: Search open issues for auth-related work
- `bd list --priority-min 0 --priority-max 1`: Critical and high priority issues only
- `bd list --created-after 2025-01-01 --status open`: Recent open issues
- `bd list --empty-description --status open`: Open issues missing descriptions
- `bd list --no-assignee --priority 1`: High priority unassigned work
- `bd list --desc-contains "TODO" --notes-contains "review"`: Find items needing attention

## Output Formats

- Default: Human-readable table
- `--json`: JSON format for scripting
- `--format digraph`: Graph format for golang.org/x/tools/cmd/digraph
- `--format dot`: Graphviz DOT format
Keine Ausgabe — alle 26 Issues in diesem Repo sind geschlossen. `bd list` zeigt standardmäßig nur offene Issues.

content.content:
○ SM-6d6 · Profilbild: Kamera-Aufnahme als Alternative zum Hochladen   [● P3 · OPEN]
Owner: tomblume · Type: feature
Created: 2026-03-02 · Updated: 2026-03-02

DESCRIPTION
Im Profilbild-Editor (SettingsPage) soll neben dem Datei-Upload auch die Gerätekamera nutzbar sein — besonders relevant auf Mobile nach PWA-Installation (SM-e3y).

UX-Flow:
  Zwei Buttons nebeneinander:
  - 'Datei hochladen' (bestehendes Verhalten, input type=file)
  - 'Foto aufnehmen' (öffnet Kamera via input type=file accept=image/* capture=environment)

Technisch:
  HTML input mit capture='environment' öffnet auf Mobile direkt die Rückkamera.
  Kein getUserMedia/MediaStream nötig — der bestehende Canvas-Editor
  (drag/zoom, PNG-Export) funktioniert identisch für beide Quellen.
  Auf Desktop wo keine Kamera verfügbar ist: Button ausblenden oder
  Browser zeigt Datei-Dialog (graceful fallback).

Abhängigkeit: profitiert von SM-e3y (PWA), ist aber kein harter Blocker.
Status: offen, noch nicht angehen.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ SM-e3y: PWA: sharemember-app installierbar machen ● P3

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/tomblume/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

## Overview

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
3. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
4. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
5. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
6. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill";
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**
- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**
- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**
- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

## After the Design

**Documentation:**
- Write the validated design to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Implementation:**
- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

**Design — Abschnitt 4: `ProfilePictureEditor` Änderungen**

Minimale Änderungen — nur zwei Ergänzungen:

2. **Neues `loadImageFromUrl`** — analog zu `handleFileChange`, aber nimmt ein `dataURL` statt ein `File`:
```typescript
const loadImageFromUrl = useCallback((dataURL: string) => {
  const img = new Image()
  img.onload = () => { /* identische Logik wie handleFileChange */ }
  img.src = dataURL
}, [])
```

3. **Zweiter Button** im `avatar-editor-buttons`-Block:
```tsx

  {t('settings.picture_camera')}
</button>
```

4. **`CameraModal`** am Ende des JSX:
```tsx
{cameraOpen && (
  <CameraModal
    onCapture={(dataURL) => { loadImageFromUrl(dataURL); setCameraOpen(false) }}
    onClose={() => setCameraOpen(false)}
  />
)}
```

---

Neue i18n-Keys (DE + EN):
```
settings.picture_camera       → "Foto aufnehmen" / "Take photo"
camera.title                  → "Foto aufnehmen" / "Take photo"
camera.capture                → "Aufnehmen" / "Capture"
camera.flip                   → "Kamera wechseln" / "Flip camera"
camera.loading                → "Kamera wird gestartet…" / "Starting camera…"
camera.error_permission       → "Kamera-Zugriff verweigert…"
camera.error_not_found        → "Keine Kamera gefunden…"
camera.error_in_use           → "Kamera wird bereits verwendet…"
camera.error_generic          → "Kamera-Fehler…"
```

---

Alles zusammen klar? Dann schreibe ich den Design-Doc und erstelle den Implementierungsplan.

input.content:
# Camera Capture for Profile Picture — Design

**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Issue**: SM-6d6
**Status**: Approved

## Overview

Add camera capture as an alternative to file upload in the `ProfilePictureEditor` on the Settings page. Works on mobile (PWA, front/back flip) and desktop (getUserMedia, multi-camera). A circular crop overlay is shown live in the video preview so the user can see the framing before capturing.

## Architecture

### New Files

```
src/components/
├── ProfilePictureEditor.tsx   (existing — minimal changes)
├── CameraModal.tsx            (new)
└── useCamera.ts               (new, adapted from shaRe-profile-picture-editor example)
```

### Data Flow

```
ProfilePictureEditor
  ├── [Datei hochladen] → input type=file → handleFileChange (unchanged)
  └── [Foto aufnehmen]  → CameraModal (open=true)
                              └── useCamera (getUserMedia, flip, stop)
                                    └── <video> + SVG circle mask overlay
                                          └── [Aufnehmen] → capturePhoto()
                                                └── onCapture(dataURL)
                                                      └── loadImageFromUrl() → Canvas editor
```

## useCamera.ts

Adapted from the example project (`shaRe-profile-picture-editor/src/hooks/useCamera.ts`). Simplifications:
- No `CanvasConfig` dependency
- No `localStorage` camera preference persistence
- No `isEnumerating` state

### Public API

```typescript
{
  isCapturing: boolean
  stream: MediaStream | null
  facingMode: 'user' | 'environment'
  availableCameras: MediaDeviceInfo[]
  cameraError: { message: string; help: string } | null
  videoRef: RefObject<HTMLVideoElement>
  captureCanvasRef: RefObject<HTMLCanvasElement>
  startCamera: () => Promise<void>
  stopCamera: () => void
  capturePhoto: () => string        // returns dataURL, stops stream
  flipCamera: () => void            // mobile: front/back, desktop: cycle cameras
  clearError: () => void
}
```

### Error Types

Handle via i18n (German/English):
- `NotAllowedError` / `PermissionDeniedError` → Kamera-Zugriff verweigert
- `NotFoundError` / `DevicesNotFoundError` → Keine Kamera gefunden
- `NotReadableError` / `TrackStartError` → Kamera wird verwendet
- default → Kamera-Fehler

## CameraModal.tsx

Modal overlay (fullscreen fixed), centred panel.

### Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│      Foto aufnehmen         │
│   ┌─────────────────────┐   │
│   │   <video>           │   │
│   │    ╭─────────╮      │   │  ← SVG circle mask (evenodd fill)
│   │    │  crop   │      │   │    outside circle: rgba(0,0,0,0.5)
│   │    ╰─────────╯      │   │
│   └─────────────────────┘   │
│  [↺ Flip] [● Aufnehmen] [✕] │
│  (error box if needed)      │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

### SVG Mask

`<svg>` positioned absolute over `<video>`, full size, `pointer-events: none`.
Uses `fill-rule="evenodd"` path: outer rectangle minus inner circle = semi-transparent overlay outside the crop area.

### Button Visibility

- **Flip**: only shown when `availableCameras.length > 1` or mobile (detected via `navigator.userAgent`)
- **Capture**: disabled while `stream === null`
- **Loading spinner**: shown over video while `stream === null`

### Props

```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
  onClose: () => void
}
```

## ProfilePictureEditor Changes

Three additions only:

1. `const [cameraOpen, setCameraOpen] = useState(false)`
2. `loadImageFromUrl(dataURL: string)` — same logic as `handleFileChange` but from dataURL instead of File
3. Second button `[Foto aufnehmen]` in `avatar-editor-buttons`
4. `{cameraOpen && <CameraModal onCapture={...} onClose={...} />}` at JSX end

## i18n Keys

| Key | DE | EN |
|-----|----|----|
| `settings.picture_camera` | Foto aufnehmen | Take photo |
| `camera.title` | Foto aufnehmen | Take photo |
| `camera.capture` | Aufnehmen | Capture |
| `camera.flip` | Kamera wechseln | Flip camera |
| `camera.loading` | Kamera wird gestartet… | Starting camera… |
| `camera.error_permission` | Kamera-Zugriff verweigert. Bitte erlaube den Kamera-Zugriff in den Browser-Einstellungen. | Camera access denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings. |
| `camera.error_not_found` | Keine Kamera gefunden. Bitte stelle sicher, dass eine Kamera angeschlossen ist. | No camera found. Please make sure a camera is connected. |
| `camera.error_in_use` | Kamera wird bereits verwendet. Schließe andere Apps und versuche es erneut. | Camera is in use. Close other apps and try again. |
| `camera.error_generic` | Kamera-Fehler. Bitte überprüfe die Berechtigungen und versuche es erneut. | Camera error. Please check permissions and try again. |

## CSS

New classes in `App.css`:
- `.camera-modal-overlay` — fullscreen fixed backdrop
- `.camera-modal` — centred card panel
- `.camera-video-wrapper` — relative container for video + SVG

toolUseResult.content:
# Camera Capture for Profile Picture — Design

**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Issue**: SM-6d6
**Status**: Approved

## Overview

Add camera capture as an alternative to file upload in the `ProfilePictureEditor` on the Settings page. Works on mobile (PWA, front/back flip) and desktop (getUserMedia, multi-camera). A circular crop overlay is shown live in the video preview so the user can see the framing before capturing.

## Architecture

### New Files

```
src/components/
├── ProfilePictureEditor.tsx   (existing — minimal changes)
├── CameraModal.tsx            (new)
└── useCamera.ts               (new, adapted from shaRe-profile-picture-editor example)
```

### Data Flow

```
ProfilePictureEditor
  ├── [Datei hochladen] → input type=file → handleFileChange (unchanged)
  └── [Foto aufnehmen]  → CameraModal (open=true)
                              └── useCamera (getUserMedia, flip, stop)
                                    └── <video> + SVG circle mask overlay
                                          └── [Aufnehmen] → capturePhoto()
                                                └── onCapture(dataURL)
                                                      └── loadImageFromUrl() → Canvas editor
```

## useCamera.ts

Adapted from the example project (`shaRe-profile-picture-editor/src/hooks/useCamera.ts`). Simplifications:
- No `CanvasConfig` dependency
- No `localStorage` camera preference persistence
- No `isEnumerating` state

### Public API

```typescript
{
  isCapturing: boolean
  stream: MediaStream | null
  facingMode: 'user' | 'environment'
  availableCameras: MediaDeviceInfo[]
  cameraError: { message: string; help: string } | null
  videoRef: RefObject<HTMLVideoElement>
  captureCanvasRef: RefObject<HTMLCanvasElement>
  startCamera: () => Promise<void>
  stopCamera: () => void
  capturePhoto: () => string        // returns dataURL, stops stream
  flipCamera: () => void            // mobile: front/back, desktop: cycle cameras
  clearError: () => void
}
```

### Error Types

Handle via i18n (German/English):
- `NotAllowedError` / `PermissionDeniedError` → Kamera-Zugriff verweigert
- `NotFoundError` / `DevicesNotFoundError` → Keine Kamera gefunden
- `NotReadableError` / `TrackStartError` → Kamera wird verwendet
- default → Kamera-Fehler

## CameraModal.tsx

Modal overlay (fullscreen fixed), centred panel.

### Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│      Foto aufnehmen         │
│   ┌─────────────────────┐   │
│   │   <video>           │   │
│   │    ╭─────────╮      │   │  ← SVG circle mask (evenodd fill)
│   │    │  crop   │      │   │    outside circle: rgba(0,0,0,0.5)
│   │    ╰─────────╯      │   │
│   └─────────────────────┘   │
│  [↺ Flip] [● Aufnehmen] [✕] │
│  (error box if needed)      │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

### SVG Mask

`<svg>` positioned absolute over `<video>`, full size, `pointer-events: none`.
Uses `fill-rule="evenodd"` path: outer rectangle minus inner circle = semi-transparent overlay outside the crop area.

### Button Visibility

- **Flip**: only shown when `availableCameras.length > 1` or mobile (detected via `navigator.userAgent`)
- **Capture**: disabled while `stream === null`
- **Loading spinner**: shown over video while `stream === null`

### Props

```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
  onClose: () => void
}
```

## ProfilePictureEditor Changes

Three additions only:

1. `const [cameraOpen, setCameraOpen] = useState(false)`
2. `loadImageFromUrl(dataURL: string)` — same logic as `handleFileChange` but from dataURL instead of File
3. Second button `[Foto aufnehmen]` in `avatar-editor-buttons`
4. `{cameraOpen && <CameraModal onCapture={...} onClose={...} />}` at JSX end

## i18n Keys

| Key | DE | EN |
|-----|----|----|
| `settings.picture_camera` | Foto aufnehmen | Take photo |
| `camera.title` | Foto aufnehmen | Take photo |
| `camera.capture` | Aufnehmen | Capture |
| `camera.flip` | Kamera wechseln | Flip camera |
| `camera.loading` | Kamera wird gestartet… | Starting camera… |
| `camera.error_permission` | Kamera-Zugriff verweigert. Bitte erlaube den Kamera-Zugriff in den Browser-Einstellungen. | Camera access denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings. |
| `camera.error_not_found` | Keine Kamera gefunden. Bitte stelle sicher, dass eine Kamera angeschlossen ist. | No camera found. Please make sure a camera is connected. |
| `camera.error_in_use` | Kamera wird bereits verwendet. Schließe andere Apps und versuche es erneut. | Camera is in use. Close other apps and try again. |
| `camera.error_generic` | Kamera-Fehler. Bitte überprüfe die Berechtigungen und versuche es erneut. | Camera error. Please check permissions and try again. |

## CSS

New classes in `App.css`:
- `.camera-modal-overlay` — fullscreen fixed backdrop
- `.camera-modal` — centred card panel
- `.camera-video-wrapper` — relative container for video + SVG
- `.camera-svg-mask` — absolute positioned overlay SVG
- `.camera-buttons` — flex row for action buttons

Styling follows existing dark theme (`#031633` background, `cta-button` classes, `border-radius: 12px`).

Option B: useCamera hook + CameraModal component with live circular
crop overlay, getUserMedia, mobile flip and desktop multi-camera support.

Base directory for this skill: /home/tomblume/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

**Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer execution choice:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/plans/<filename>.md`. Two execution options:**

**1. Subagent-Driven (this session)** - I dispatch fresh subagent per task, review between tasks, fast iteration

**2. Parallel Session (separate)** - Open new session with executing-plans, batch execution with checkpoints

**Which approach?"**

**If Subagent-Driven chosen:**
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Stay in this session
- Fresh subagent per task + code review

**If Parallel Session chosen:**
- Guide them to open new session in worktree
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** New session uses superpowers:executing-plans

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/tomblume/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/subagent-driven-development

# Subagent-Driven Development

Execute plan by dispatching fresh subagent per task, with two-stage review after each: spec compliance review first, then code quality review.

**Core principle:** Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review (spec then quality) = high quality, fast iteration

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Have implementation plan?" [shape=diamond];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Stay in this session?" [shape=diamond];
    "subagent-driven-development" [shape=box];
    "executing-plans" [shape=box];
    "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [shape=box];

    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Tasks mostly independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Have implementation plan?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Stay in this session?" [label="yes"];
    "Tasks mostly independent?" -> "Manual execution or brainstorm first" [label="no - tightly coupled"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "subagent-driven-development" [label="yes"];
    "Stay in this session?" -> "executing-plans" [label="no - parallel session"];
}
```

**vs. Executing Plans (parallel session):**
- Same session (no context switch)
- Fresh subagent per task (no context pollution)
- Two-stage review after each task: spec compliance first, then code quality
- Faster iteration (no human-in-loop between tasks)

## The Process

```dot
digraph process {
    rankdir=TB;

    subgraph cluster_per_task {
        label="Per Task";
        "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent asks questions?" [shape=diamond];
        "Answer questions, provide context" [shape=box];
        "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [shape=box];
        "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [shape=box];
        "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" [shape=diamond];
        "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [shape=box];
        "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    }

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" [shape=box];
    "More tasks remain?" [shape=diamond];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [shape=box];
    "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch" [shape=box style=filled fillcolor=lightgreen];

    "Read plan, extract all tasks with full text, note context, create TodoWrite" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" -> "Implementer subagent asks questions?";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Answer questions, provide context" [label="yes"];
    "Answer questions, provide context" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)";
    "Implementer subagent asks questions?" -> "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent implements, tests, commits, self-reviews" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?";
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes spec gaps" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer subagent (./spec-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Spec reviewer subagent confirms code matches spec?" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" -> "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?";
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" [label="no"];
    "Implementer subagent fixes quality issues" -> "Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent (./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md)" [label="re-review"];
    "Code quality reviewer subagent approves?" -> "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" [label="yes"];
    "Mark task complete in TodoWrite" -> "More tasks remain?";
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch implementer subagent (./implementer-prompt.md)" [label="yes"];
    "More tasks remain?" -> "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" [label="no"];
    "Dispatch final code reviewer subagent for entire implementation" -> "Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch";
}
```

## Prompt Templates

- `./implementer-prompt.md` - Dispatch implementer subagent
- `./spec-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch spec compliance reviewer subagent
- `./code-quality-reviewer-prompt.md` - Dispatch code quality reviewer subagent

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using Subagent-Driven Development to execute this plan.

[Read plan file once: docs/plans/feature-plan.md]
[Extract all 5 tasks with full text and context]
[Create TodoWrite with all tasks]

Task 1: Hook installation script

[Get Task 1 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: "Before I begin - should the hook be installed at user or system level?"

You: "User level (~/.config/superpowers/hooks/)"

Implementer: "Got it. Implementing now..."
[Later] Implementer:
  - Implemented install-hook command
  - Added tests, 5/5 passing
  - Self-review: Found I missed --force flag, added it
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant - all requirements met, nothing extra

[Get git SHAs, dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Good test coverage, clean. Issues: None. Approved.

[Mark Task 1 complete]

Task 2: Recovery modes

[Get Task 2 text and context (already extracted)]
[Dispatch implementation subagent with full task text + context]

Implementer: [No questions, proceeds]
Implementer:
  - Added verify/repair modes
  - 8/8 tests passing
  - Self-review: All good
  - Committed

[Dispatch spec compliance reviewer]
Spec reviewer: ❌ Issues:
  - Missing: Progress reporting (spec says "report every 100 items")
  - Extra: Added --json flag (not requested)

[Implementer fixes issues]
Implementer: Removed --json flag, added progress reporting

[Spec reviewer reviews again]
Spec reviewer: ✅ Spec compliant now

[Dispatch code quality reviewer]
Code reviewer: Strengths: Solid. Issues (Important): Magic number (100)

[Implementer fixes]
Implementer: Extracted PROGRESS_INTERVAL constant

[Code reviewer reviews again]
Code reviewer: ✅ Approved

[Mark Task 2 complete]

...

[After all tasks]
[Dispatch final code-reviewer]
Final reviewer: All requirements met, ready to merge

Done!
```

## Advantages

**vs. Manual execution:**
- Subagents follow TDD naturally
- Fresh context per task (no confusion)
- Parallel-safe (subagents don't interfere)
- Subagent can ask questions (before AND during work)

**vs. Executing Plans:**
- Same session (no handoff)
- Continuous progress (no waiting)
- Review checkpoints automatic

**Efficiency gains:**
- No file reading overhead (controller provides full text)
- Controller curates exactly what context is needed
- Subagent gets complete information upfront
- Questions surfaced before work begins (not after)

**Quality gates:**
- Self-review catches issues before handoff
- Two-stage review: spec compliance, then code quality
- Review loops ensure fixes actually work
- Spec compliance prevents over/under-building
- Code quality ensures implementation is well-built

**Cost:**
- More subagent invocations (implementer + 2 reviewers per task)
- Controller does more prep work (extracting all tasks upfront)
- Review loops add iterations
- But catches issues early (cheaper than debugging later)

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent
- Skip reviews (spec compliance OR code quality)
- Proceed with unfixed issues
- Dispatch multiple implementation subagents in parallel (conflicts)
- Make subagent read plan file (provide full text instead)
- Skip scene-setting context (subagent needs to understand where task fits)
- Ignore subagent questions (answer before letting them proceed)
- Accept "close enough" on spec compliance (spec reviewer found issues = not done)
- Skip review loops (reviewer found issues = implementer fixes = review again)
- Let implementer self-review replace actual review (both are needed)
- **Start code quality review before spec compliance is ✅** (wrong order)
- Move to next task while either review has open issues

**If subagent asks questions:**
- Answer clearly and completely
- Provide additional context if needed
- Don't rush them into implementation

**If reviewer finds issues:**
- Implementer (same subagent) fixes them
- Reviewer reviews again
- Repeat until approved
- Don't skip the re-review

**If subagent fails task:**
- Dispatch fix subagent with specific instructions
- Don't try to fix manually (context pollution)

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:requesting-code-review** - Code review template for reviewer subagents
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

**Subagents should use:**
- **superpowers:test-driven-development** - Subagents follow TDD for each task

**Alternative workflow:**
- **superpowers:executing-plans** - Use for parallel session instead of same-session execution

file.content:
# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - What you implemented
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns
```

file.content:
# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - ✅ Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - ❌ Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```

    "error_generic": "Kamera-Fehler. Bitte überprüfe die Berechtigungen und versuche es erneut."
  }
```

**Step 2: Add keys to `en.json`** same structure:

```json
    "picture_camera": "Take photo",

  "camera": {
    "title": "Take photo",
    "capture": "Capture",
    "flip": "Flip camera",
    "loading": "Starting camera…",

```bash
git add src/i18n/locales/de.json src/i18n/locales/en.json

```

- Project: `sharemember-app` at `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app`
- This is a React/TypeScript app with react-i18next
- The `settings` namespace is a flat key object inside `de.json` / `en.json` at the top level key `"settings"`
- `"camera"` will be a new top-level namespace (sibling to `"settings"`, `"verify"`, etc.)
- The JSON files must remain valid JSON — be careful with trailing commas
- Current last line of `de.json` is `}` (closes the root object) — the `"camera"` block goes before it, with a comma after the previous last key

If you have any questions about the requirements, ask now before starting.

Review the i18n key additions for Task 1 of the camera capture feature in sharemember-app.

WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Added `"picture_camera"` key to the `settings` namespace and a new top-level `"camera"` namespace with 8 error/UI string keys to both `src/i18n/locales/de.json` and `src/i18n/locales/en.json`.

PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 1 from docs/plans/2026-03-03-camera-capture.md in /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app

BASE_SHA: 54847b20fe2eed656232505d21739903135d0f20
HEAD_SHA: run `cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && git rev-parse HEAD` to get current

DESCRIPTION: i18n keys for camera capture feature — new `camera` namespace + `settings.picture_camera` key in DE and EN locale files.

Focus on: key naming consistency with existing patterns, completeness of error coverage, translation quality.

1→# Camera Capture for Profile Picture — Implementation Plan 2→ 3→> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. 4→ 5→**Goal:** Add a "Foto aufnehmen" button to the profile picture editor that opens a camera modal with live video, a circular crop overlay, and front/back flip support on mobile and multi-camera on desktop. 6→ 7→**Architecture:** New `useCamera.ts` hook manages getUserMedia stream state (adapted from `shaRe-profile-picture-editor/src/hooks/useCamera.ts`). New `CameraModal.tsx` renders the video preview + SVG circular mask + buttons. `ProfilePictureEditor.tsx` adds a second button and a `loadImageFromUrl()` helper — the captured dataURL flows through the same canvas editor path as file upload. 8→ 9→**Tech Stack:** React, TypeScript, getUserMedia API, SVG, react-i18next, existing CSS dark theme 10→ 11→--- 12→ 13→### Task 1: i18n keys 14→ 15→**Files:** 16→- Modify: `src/i18n/locales/de.json` (after line 83, inside `settings` object) 17→- Modify: `src/i18n/locales/en.json` (after line 83, inside `settings` object) 18→ 19→**Step 1: Add keys to `de.json`** after `"picture_size_error"` line: 20→ 21→```json 22→ "picture_camera": "Foto aufnehmen", 23→``` 24→ 25→Then add a top-level `"camera"` key at the end of the root object (before the closing `}`): 26→ 27→```json 28→ "camera": { 29→ "title": "Foto aufnehmen", 30→ "capture": "Aufnehmen", 31→ "flip": "Kamera wechseln", 32→ "loading": "Kamera wird gestartet…", 33→ "error_permission": "Kamera-Zugriff verweigert. Bitte erlaube den Kamera-Zugriff in den Browser-Einstellungen.", 34→ "error_not_found": "Keine Kamera gefunden. Bitte stelle sicher, dass eine Kamera angeschlossen ist.", 35→ "error_in_use": "Kamera wird bereits verwendet. Schließe andere Apps und versuche es erneut.", 36→ "error_generic": "Kamera-Fehler. Bitte überprüfe die Berechtigungen und versuche es erneut." 37→ } 38→``` 39→ 40→**Step 2: Add keys to `en.json`** same structure: 41→ 42→```json 43→ "picture_camera": "Take photo", 44→``` 45→ 46→```json 47→ "camera": { 48→ "title": "Take photo", 49→ "capture": "Capture", 50→ "flip": "Flip camera", 51→ "loading": "Starting camera…", 52→ "error_permission": "Camera access denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings.", 53→ "error_not_found": "No camera found. Please make sure a camera is connected.", 54→ "error_in_use": "Camera is in use. Close other apps and try again.", 55→ "error_generic": "Camera error. Please check permissions and try again." 56→ } 57→``` 58→ 59→**Step 3: Verify** — run `npm run build` (no TypeScript errors, no missing key warnings): 60→```bash 61→cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5 62→``` 63→Expected: `✓ built in ...` 64→ 65→**Step 4: Commit** 66→```bash 67→git add src/i18n/locales/de.json src/i18n/locales/en.json 68→git commit -m "feat(SM-6d6): add camera i18n keys (DE + EN)" 69→``` 70→ 71→--- 72→ 73→### Task 2: `useCamera.ts` hook 74→ 75→**Files:** 76→- Create: `src/useCamera.ts` 77→ 78→Adapted from `shaRe-profile-picture-editor/src/hooks/useCamera.ts`. Key simplifications: no `CanvasConfig`, no `localStorage`, no `isEnumerating`. 79→ 80→**Step 1: Create `src/useCamera.ts`:** 81→ 82→```typescript 83→import { useState, useRef, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react' 84→ 85→interface CameraError { 86→ message: string 87→ help: string 88→} 89→ 90→const getErrorDetails = (errorName: string): CameraError => { 91→ switch (errorName) { 92→ case 'NotAllowedError': 93→ case 'PermissionDeniedError': 94→ return { message: 'error_permission', help: '' } 95→ case 'NotFoundError': 96→ case 'DevicesNotFoundError': 97→ return { message: 'error_not_found', help: '' } 98→ case 'NotReadableError': 99→ case 'TrackStartError': 100→ return { message: 'error_in_use', help: '' } 101→ default: 102→ return { message: 'error_generic', help: '' } 103→ } 104→} 105→ 106→const isMobile = (): boolean => 107→ /Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(navigator.userAgent) 108→ 109→export function useCamera() { 110→ const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false) 111→ const [stream, setStream] = useState(null) 112→ const [facingMode, setFacingMode] = useState<'user' | 'environment'>('environment') 113→ const [availableCameras, setAvailableCameras] = useState([]) 114→ const [selectedCameraId, setSelectedCameraId] = useState(null) 115→ const [cameraError, setCameraError] = useState(null) 116→ 117→ const videoRef = useRef(null) 118→ const captureCanvasRef = useRef(null) 119→ 120→ // Enumerate cameras after first getUserMedia call (labels only available after permission) 121→ const enumerateCameras = useCallback(async () => { 122→ if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.enumerateDevices) return 123→ const devices = await navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices() 124→ const cameras = devices.filter((d) => d.kind === 'videoinput') 125→ setAvailableCameras(cameras) 126→ }, []) 127→ 128→ const startCamera = useCallback(async (deviceId?: string) => { 129→ setIsCapturing(true) 130→ setCameraError(null) 131→ 132→ try { 133→ if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia) { 134→ throw Object.assign(new Error(), { name: 'NotSupportedError' }) 135→ } 136→ 137→ const videoConstraints: MediaTrackConstraints = deviceId 138→ ? { deviceId: { exact: deviceId } } 139→ : isMobile() 140→ ? { facingMode } 141→ : { width: { ideal: 1280 }, height: { ideal: 720 } } 142→ 143→ const mediaStream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: videoConstraints }) 144→ setStream(mediaStream) 145→ await enumerateCameras() 146→ 147→ if (videoRef.current) { 148→ videoRef.current.srcObject = mediaStream 149→ await videoRef.current.play() 150→ } 151→ } catch (err: any) { 152→ setIsCapturing(false) 153→ setCameraError(getErrorDetails(err?.name ?? '')) 154→ } 155→ }, [facingMode, enumerateCameras]) 156→ 157→ const stopCamera = useCallback(() => { 158→ stream?.getTracks().forEach((t) => t.stop()) 159→ setStream(null) 160→ setIsCapturing(false) 161→ setCameraError(null) 162→ if (videoRef.current) videoRef.current.srcObject = null 163→ }, [stream]) 164→ 165→ const capturePhoto = useCallback((): string => { 166→ const video = videoRef.current 167→ const canvas = captureCanvasRef.current 168→ if (!video || !canvas) return '' 169→ canvas.width = video.videoWidth 170→ canvas.height = video.videoHeight 171→ canvas.getContext('2d')?.drawImage(video, 0, 0) 172→ const dataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.9) 173→ stopCamera() 174→ return dataURL 175→ }, [stopCamera]) 176→ 177→ const flipCamera = useCallback(() => { 178→ if (!isCapturing) return 179→ stopCamera() 180→ if (isMobile()) { 181→ const next = facingMode === 'environment' ? 'user' : 'environment' 182→ setFacingMode(next) 183→ setTimeout(() => void startCamera(), 150) 184→ } else { 185→ const currentIdx = availableCameras.findIndex((c) => c.deviceId === selectedCameraId) 186→ const next = availableCameras[(currentIdx + 1) % availableCameras.length] 187→ if (next) { 188→ setSelectedCameraId(next.deviceId) 189→ setTimeout(() => void startCamera(next.deviceId), 150) 190→ } 191→ } 192→ }, [isCapturing, facingMode, availableCameras, selectedCameraId, stopCamera, startCamera]) 193→ 194→ // Auto-stop on unmount 195→ useEffect(() => { 196→ return () => { 197→ stream?.getTracks().forEach((t) => t.stop()) 198→ } 199→ }, [stream]) 200→ 201→ const clearError = useCallback(() => setCameraError(null), []) 202→ 203→ const showFlip = isMobile() || availableCameras.length > 1 204→ 205→ return { 206→ isCapturing, 207→ stream, 208→ facingMode, 209→ availableCameras, 210→ cameraError, 211→ videoRef, 212→ captureCanvasRef, 213→ startCamera, 214→ stopCamera, 215→ capturePhoto, 216→ flipCamera, 217→ clearError, 218→ showFlip, 219→ } 220→} 221→``` 222→ 223→**Step 2: Verify TypeScript compiles:** 224→```bash 225→cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 226→``` 227→Expected: no errors 228→ 229→**Step 3: Commit** 230→```bash 231→git add src/useCamera.ts 232→git commit -m "feat(SM-6d6): add useCamera hook (getUserMedia, flip, enumerate)" 233→``` 234→ 235→--- 236→ 237→### Task 3: CSS for CameraModal 238→ 239→**Files:** 240→- Modify: `src/App.css` (append at end) 241→ 242→**Step 1: Append to `src/App.css`:** 243→ 244→```css 245→/* ── Camera Modal ──────────────────────────────── */ 246→.camera-modal-overlay { 247→ position: fixed; 248→ inset: 0; 249→ background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8); 250→ display: flex; 251→ align-items: center; 252→ justify-content: center; 253→ z-index: 1000; 254→} 255→ 256→.camera-modal { 257→ background: #031633;
