1→import { useState, useRef, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→
     3→interface CameraError {
     4→  message: string
     5→  help: string
     6→}
     7→
     8→const getErrorDetails = (errorName: string): CameraError => {
     9→  switch (errorName) {
    10→    case 'NotAllowedError':
    11→    case 'PermissionDeniedError':
    12→      return { message: 'error_permission', help: '' }
    13→    case 'NotFoundError':
    14→    case 'DevicesNotFoundError':
    15→      return { message: 'error_not_found', help: '' }
    16→    case 'NotReadableError':
    17→    case 'TrackStartError':
    18→      return { message: 'error_in_use', help: '' }
    19→    default:
    20→      return { message: 'error_generic', help: '' }
    21→  }
    22→}
    23→
    24→const isMobile = (): boolean =>
    25→  /Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(
    26→    navigator.userAgent
    27→  )
    28→
    29→export function useCamera() {
    30→  const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false)
    31→  const [stream, setStream] = useState<MediaStream | null>(null)
    32→  const [facingMode, setFacingMode] = useState<'user' | 'environment'>(
    33→    'environment'
    34→  )
    35→  const [availableCameras, setAvailableCameras] = useState<MediaDeviceInfo[]>(
    36→    []
    37→  )
    38→  const [selectedCameraId, setSelectedCameraId] = useState<string | null>(null)
    39→  const [cameraError, setCameraError] = useState<CameraError | null>(null)
    40→
    41→  const videoRef = useRef<HTMLVideoElement>(null)
    42→  const captureCanvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null)
    43→
    44→  // Enumerate cameras after first getUserMedia call (labels only available after permission)
    45→  const enumerateCameras = useCallback(async () => {
    46→    if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.enumerateDevices) return
    47→    const devices = await navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()
    48→    const cameras = devices.filter(d => d.kind === 'videoinput')
    49→    setAvailableCameras(cameras)
    50→  }, [])
    51→
    52→  const startCamera = useCallback(
    53→    async (deviceId?: string, facingModeOverride?: 'user' | 'environment') => {
    54→      setIsCapturing(true)
    55→      setCameraError(null)
    56→
    57→      try {
    58→        if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia) {
    59→          throw Object.assign(new Error(), { name: 'NotSupportedError' })
    60→        }
    61→
    62→        const videoConstraints: MediaTrackConstraints = deviceId
    63→          ? { deviceId: { exact: deviceId } }
    64→          : isMobile()
    65→            ? { facingMode: facingModeOverride ?? facingMode }
    66→            : { width: { ideal: 1280 }, height: { ideal: 720 } }
    67→
    68→        const mediaStream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({
    69→          video: videoConstraints,
    70→        })
    71→        setStream(mediaStream)
    72→
    73→        // Sync selectedCameraId to the actual active camera
    74→        const videoTrack = mediaStream.getVideoTracks()[0]
    75→        if (videoTrack) {
    76→          const actualDeviceId = videoTrack.getSettings().deviceId
    77→          if (actualDeviceId) setSelectedCameraId(actualDeviceId)
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        await enumerateCameras()
    81→
    82→        if (videoRef.current) {
    83→          videoRef.current.srcObject = mediaStream
    84→          await videoRef.current.play()
    85→        }
    86→      } catch (err: any) {
    87→        setIsCapturing(false)
    88→        setCameraError(getErrorDetails(err?.name ?? ''))
    89→      }
    90→    },
    91→    [facingMode, enumerateCameras]
    92→  )
    93→
    94→  const stopCamera = useCallback(() => {
    95→    stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
    96→    setStream(null)
    97→    setIsCapturing(false)
    98→    setCameraError(null)
    99→    if (videoRef.current) videoRef.current.srcObject = null
   100→  }, [stream])
   101→
   102→  const capturePhoto = useCallback((): string => {
   103→    const video = videoRef.current
   104→    const canvas = captureCanvasRef.current
   105→    if (!video || !canvas) return ''
   106→    canvas.width = video.videoWidth
   107→    canvas.height = video.videoHeight
   108→    canvas.getContext('2d')?.drawImage(video, 0, 0)
   109→    const dataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.9)
   110→    stopCamera()
   111→    return dataURL
   112→  }, [stopCamera])
   113→
   114→  const flipCamera = useCallback(() => {
   115→    if (!isCapturing) return
   116→    stopCamera()
   117→    if (isMobile()) {
   118→      const next = facingMode === 'environment' ? 'user' : 'environment'
   119→      setFacingMode(next)
   120→      setTimeout(() => void startCamera(undefined, next), 150)
   121→    } else {
   122→      const currentIdx = availableCameras.findIndex(
   123→        c => c.deviceId === selectedCameraId
   124→      )
   125→      const next = availableCameras[(currentIdx + 1) % availableCameras.length]
   126→      if (next) {
   127→        setSelectedCameraId(next.deviceId)
   128→        setTimeout(() => void startCamera(next.deviceId), 150)
   129→      }
   130→    }
   131→  }, [
   132→    isCapturing,
   133→    facingMode,
   134→    availableCameras,
   135→    selectedCameraId,
   136→    stopCamera,
   137→    startCamera,
   138→  ])

New `CameraModal.tsx` renders the video preview + SVG circular mask + buttons. `ProfilePictureEditor.tsx` adds a second button and a `loadImageFromUrl()` helper — the captured dataURL flows through the same canvas editor path as file upload. 8→ 9→**Tech Stack:** React, TypeScript, getUserMedia API, SVG, react-i18next, existing CSS dark theme 10→ 11→--- 12→ 13→### Task 1: i18n keys 14→ 15→**Files:** 16→- Modify: `src/i18n/locales/de.json` (after line 83, inside `settings` object) 17→- Modify: `src/i18n/locales/en.json` (after line 83, inside `settings` object) 18→ 19→**Step 1: Add keys to `de.json`** after `"picture_size_error"` line: 20→ 21→```json 22→ "picture_camera": "Foto aufnehmen", 23→``` 24→ 25→Then add a top-level `"camera"` key at the end of the root object (before the closing `}`): 26→ 27→```json 28→ "camera": { 29→ "title": "Foto aufnehmen", 30→ "capture": "Aufnehmen", 31→ "flip": "Kamera wechseln", 32→ "loading": "Kamera wird gestartet…", 33→ "error_permission": "Kamera-Zugriff verweigert. Bitte überprüfe die Berechtigungen und versuche es erneut." 37→ } 38→``` 39→ 40→**Step 2: Add keys to `en.json`** same structure: 41→ 42→```json 43→ "picture_camera": "Take photo", 44→``` 45→ 46→```json 47→ "camera": { 48→ "title": "Take photo", 49→ "capture": "Capture", 50→ "flip": "Flip camera", 51→ "loading": "Starting camera…", 52→ "error_permission": "Camera access denied. Key simplifications: no `CanvasConfig`, no `localStorage`, no `isEnumerating`. 79→ 80→**Step 1: Create `src/useCamera.ts`:** 81→ 82→```typescript 83→import { useState, useRef, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react' 84→ 85→interface CameraError { 86→ message: string 87→ help: string 88→} 89→ 90→const getErrorDetails = (errorName: string): CameraError => { 91→ switch (errorName) { 92→ case 'NotAllowedError': 93→ case 'PermissionDeniedError': 94→ return { message: 'error_permission', help: '' } 95→ case 'NotFoundError': 96→ case 'DevicesNotFoundError': 97→ return { message: 'error_not_found', help: '' } 98→ case 'NotReadableError': 99→ case 'TrackStartError': 100→ return { message: 'error_in_use', help: '' } 101→ default: 102→ return { message: 'error_generic', help: '' } 103→ } 104→} 105→ 106→const isMobile = (): boolean => 107→ /Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(navigator.userAgent) 108→ 109→export function useCamera() { 110→ const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false) 111→ const [stream, setStream] = useState(null) 112→ const [facingMode, setFacingMode] = useState<'user' | 'environment'>('environment') 113→ const [availableCameras, setAvailableCameras] = useState([]) 114→ const [selectedCameraId, setSelectedCameraId] = useState(null) 115→ const [cameraError, setCameraError] = useState(null) 116→ 117→ const videoRef = useRef(null) 118→ const captureCanvasRef = useRef(null) 119→ 120→ // Enumerate cameras after first getUserMedia call (labels only available after permission) 121→ const enumerateCameras = useCallback(async () => { 122→ if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.enumerateDevices) return 123→ const devices = await navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices() 124→ const cameras = devices.filter((d) => d.kind === 'videoinput') 125→ setAvailableCameras(cameras) 126→ }, []) 127→ 128→ const startCamera = useCallback(async (deviceId?: string) => { 129→ setIsCapturing(true) 130→ setCameraError(null) 131→ 132→ try { 133→ if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia) { 134→ throw Object.assign(new Error(), { name: 'NotSupportedError' }) 135→ } 136→ 137→ const videoConstraints: MediaTrackConstraints = deviceId 138→ ? { deviceId: { exact: deviceId } } 139→ : isMobile() 140→ ? { facingMode } 141→ : { width: { ideal: 1280 }, height: { ideal: 720 } } 142→ 143→ const mediaStream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: videoConstraints }) 144→ setStream(mediaStream) 145→ await enumerateCameras() 146→ 147→ if (videoRef.current) { 148→ videoRef.current.srcObject = mediaStream 149→ await videoRef.current.play() 150→ } 151→ } catch (err: any) { 152→ setIsCapturing(false) 153→ setCameraError(getErrorDetails(err?.name ?? '')) 154→ } 155→ }, [facingMode, enumerateCameras]) 156→ 157→ const stopCamera = useCallback(() => { 158→ stream?.getTracks().forEach((t) => t.stop()) 159→ setStream(null) 160→ setIsCapturing(false) 161→ setCameraError(null) 162→ if (videoRef.current) videoRef.current.srcObject = null 163→ }, [stream]) 164→ 165→ const capturePhoto = useCallback((): string => { 166→ const video = videoRef.current 167→ const canvas = captureCanvasRef.current 168→ if (!video || !canvas) return '' 169→ canvas.width = video.videoWidth 170→ canvas.height = video.videoHeight 171→ canvas.getContext('2d')?.drawImage(video, 0, 0) 172→ const dataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.9) 173→ stopCamera() 174→ return dataURL 175→ }, [stopCamera]) 176→ 177→ const flipCamera = useCallback(() => { 178→ if (!isCapturing) return 179→ stopCamera() 180→ if (isMobile()) { 181→ const next = facingMode === 'environment' ? 'user' : 'environment' 182→ setFacingMode(next) 183→ setTimeout(() => void startCamera(), 150) 184→ } else { 185→ const currentIdx = availableCameras.findIndex((c) => c.deviceId === selectedCameraId) 186→ const next = availableCameras[(currentIdx + 1) % availableCameras.length] 187→ if (next) { 188→ setSelectedCameraId(next.deviceId) 189→ setTimeout(() => void startCamera(next.deviceId), 150) 190→ } 191→ } 192→ }, [isCapturing, facingMode, availableCameras, selectedCameraId, stopCamera, startCamera]) 193→ 194→ // Auto-stop on unmount 195→ useEffect(() => { 196→ return () => { 197→ stream?.getTracks().forEach((t) => t.stop()) 198→ } 199→ }, [stream]) 200→ 201→ const clearError = useCallback(() => setCameraError(null), []) 202→ 203→ const showFlip = isMobile() || availableCameras.length > 1 204→ 205→ return { 206→ isCapturing, 207→ stream, 208→ facingMode, 209→ availableCameras, 210→ cameraError, 211→ videoRef, 212→ captureCanvasRef, 213→ startCamera, 214→ stopCamera, 215→ capturePhoto, 216→ flipCamera, 217→ clearError, 218→ showFlip, 219→ } 220→} 221→``` 222→ 223→**Step 2: Verify TypeScript compiles:** 224→```bash 225→cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20 226→``` 227→Expected: no errors 228→ 229→**Step 3: Commit** 230→```bash 231→git add src/useCamera.ts 232→git commit -m "feat(SM-6d6): add useCamera hook (getUserMedia, flip, enumerate)" 233→``` 234→ 235→--- 236→ 237→### Task 3: CSS for CameraModal 238→ 239→**Files:** 240→- Modify: `src/App.css` (append at end) 241→ 242→**Step 1: Append to `src/App.css`:** 243→ 244→```css 245→/* ── Camera Modal ──────────────────────────────── */ 246→.camera-modal-overlay { 247→ position: fixed; 1→import { useState, useRef, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→
     3→interface CameraError {
     4→  message: string
     5→  help: string
     6→}
     7→
     8→const getErrorDetails = (errorName: string): CameraError => {
     9→  switch (errorName) {
    10→    case 'NotAllowedError':
    11→    case 'PermissionDeniedError':
    12→      return { message: 'error_permission', help: '' }
    13→    case 'NotFoundError':
    14→    case 'DevicesNotFoundError':
    15→      return { message: 'error_not_found', help: '' }
    16→    case 'NotReadableError':
    17→    case 'TrackStartError':
    18→      return { message: 'error_in_use', help: '' }
    19→    default:
    20→      return { message: 'error_generic', help: '' }
    21→  }
    22→}
    23→
    24→const isMobile = (): boolean =>
    25→  /Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(
    26→    navigator.userAgent
    27→  )
    28→
    29→export function useCamera() {
    30→  const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false)
    31→  const [stream, setStream] = useState<MediaStream | null>(null)
    32→  const [facingMode, setFacingMode] = useState<'user' | 'environment'>(
    33→    'environment'
    34→  )
    35→  const [availableCameras, setAvailableCameras] = useState<MediaDeviceInfo[]>(
    36→    []
    37→  )
    38→  const [selectedCameraId, setSelectedCameraId] = useState<string | null>(null)
    39→  const [cameraError, setCameraError] = useState<CameraError | null>(null)
    40→
    41→  const videoRef = useRef<HTMLVideoElement>(null)
    42→  const captureCanvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null)
    43→
    44→  // Enumerate cameras after first getUserMedia call (labels only available after permission)
    45→  const enumerateCameras = useCallback(async () => {
    46→    if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.enumerateDevices) return
    47→    const devices = await navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()
    48→    const cameras = devices.filter(d => d.kind === 'videoinput')
    49→    setAvailableCameras(cameras)
    50→  }, [])
    51→
    52→  const startCamera = useCallback(
    53→    async (deviceId?: string, facingModeOverride?: 'user' | 'environment') => {
    54→      setIsCapturing(true)
    55→      setCameraError(null)
    56→
    57→      try {
    58→        if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia) {
    59→          throw Object.assign(new Error(), { name: 'NotSupportedError' })
    60→        }
    61→
    62→        const videoConstraints: MediaTrackConstraints = deviceId
    63→          ? { deviceId: { exact: deviceId } }
    64→          : isMobile()
    65→            ? { facingMode: facingModeOverride ?? facingMode }
    66→            : { width: { ideal: 1280 }, height: { ideal: 720 } }
    67→
    68→        const mediaStream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({
    69→          video: videoConstraints,
    70→        })
    71→        setStream(mediaStream)
    72→
    73→        // Sync selectedCameraId to the actual active camera
    74→        const videoTrack = mediaStream.getVideoTracks()[0]
    75→        if (videoTrack) {
    76→          const actualDeviceId = videoTrack.getSettings().deviceId
    77→          if (actualDeviceId) setSelectedCameraId(actualDeviceId)
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        await enumerateCameras()
    81→
    82→        if (videoRef.current) {
    83→          videoRef.current.srcObject = mediaStream
    84→          await videoRef.current.play()
    85→        }
    86→      } catch (err: any) {
    87→        setIsCapturing(false)
    88→        setCameraError(getErrorDetails(err?.name ?? ''))
    89→      }
    90→    },
    91→    [facingMode, enumerateCameras]
    92→  )
    93→
    94→  const stopCamera = useCallback(() => {
    95→    stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
    96→    setStream(null)
    97→    setIsCapturing(false)
    98→    setCameraError(null)
    99→    if (videoRef.current) videoRef.current.srcObject = null
   100→  }, [stream])
   101→
   102→  const capturePhoto = useCallback((): string => {
   103→    const video = videoRef.current
   104→    const canvas = captureCanvasRef.current
   105→    if (!video || !canvas) return ''
   106→    canvas.width = video.videoWidth
   107→    canvas.height = video.videoHeight
   108→    canvas.getContext('2d')?.drawImage(video, 0, 0)
   109→    const dataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.9)
   110→    stopCamera()
   111→    return dataURL
   112→  }, [stopCamera])
   113→
   114→  const flipCamera = useCallback(() => {
   115→    if (!isCapturing) return
   116→    stopCamera()
   117→    if (isMobile()) {
   118→      const next = facingMode === 'environment' ? 'user' : 'environment'
   119→      setFacingMode(next)
   120→      setTimeout(() => void startCamera(undefined, next), 150)
   121→    } else {
   122→      const currentIdx = availableCameras.findIndex(
   123→        c => c.deviceId === selectedCameraId
   124→      )
   125→      const next = availableCameras[(currentIdx + 1) % availableCameras.length]
   126→      if (next) {
   127→        setSelectedCameraId(next.deviceId)
   128→        setTimeout(() => void startCamera(next.deviceId), 150)
   129→      }
   130→    }
   131→  }, [
   132→    isCapturing,
   133→    facingMode,
   134→    availableCameras,
   135→    selectedCameraId,
   136→    stopCamera,
   137→    startCamera,
   138→  ])
   139→
   140→  // Auto-stop on unmount
   141→  useEffect(() => {
   142→    return () => {
   143→      stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
   144→    }
   145→  }, [stream])
   146→
   147→  const clearError = useCallback(() => setCameraError(null), [])
   148→
   149→  const showFlip = isMobile() || availableCameras.length > 1
   150→
   151→  return {
   152→    isCapturing,
   153→    stream,
   154→    facingMode,
   155→    availableCameras,
   156→    cameraError,
   157→    videoRef,
   158→    captureCanvasRef,
   159→    startCamera,
   160→    stopCamera,
   161→    capturePhoto,
   162→    flipCamera,
   163→    clearError,
   164→    showFlip,
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→

import math
# SVG arc path: M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0
# Start: (50,5) + move(-40,40) = (10, 45)
# First arc: from (10,45), rx=ry=40, sweep=1, large-arc=0, dx=80, dy=0 -> endpoint=(90,45)
# Second arc: from (90,45), rx=ry=40, sweep=0, large-arc=1, dx=-80, dy=0 -> endpoint=(10,45)
# The center of a circle through these: for arc 1 (sweep=1 means clockwise SVG)
# midpoint = (50, 45), distance from center = 40
# perpendicular: up or down by sqrt(40^2 - 40^2) = 0 => the two points ARE the diameter!
# So center = (50, 45), radius = 40 => circle top = 5, bottom = 85, left = 10, right = 90
# But viewBox is 0-100, so center should be at 50,50 for a centered circle
print('Arc center Y:', 45)
print('Circle top:', 45-40, 'Circle bottom:', 45+40)
print('Expected center Y for viewBox 100x100:', 50)
print('Vertical offset from center:', 50-45, 'pixels in viewBox units')
" 2>&1

# The SVG path is: M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0
# Let us trace the arc subpath carefully:
# After 'M50,5 m-40,40' we are at absolute position (10, 45)
# Arc: a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 — relative arc, so endpoint is (10+80, 45+0) = (90, 45)
# large-arc-flag=1, sweep-flag=0 (counter-clockwise in SVG)
# For two points (10,45) and (90,45) with r=40, the possible centers:
# midpoint (50,45), distance between points = 80, half = 40
# distance from midpoint to center = sqrt(40^2 - 40^2) = 0
# So the only possible center is (50, 45)!
# Circle: center (50,45), r=40 — top at y=5, bottom at y=85
# The <circle> element uses cx=50 cy=50 r=40 — center at (50,50)
# So the SVG mask arc circle and the stroke circle are offset by 5 units in Y
print('SVG path circle center: (50, 45)')
print('SVG stroke circle center: (50, 50)')
print('Mismatch: 5 viewBox units vertically')
print()

print('  Starting at (10,50), arc to (90,50), back to (10,50)')
print('  Both points on horizontal diameter, center = (50,50) - correct')
" 2>&1

input.command:
python3 -c "
# Check the CSS: camera-video-wrapper has aspect-ratio: 4 / 3
# SVG has preserveAspectRatio='none' and fills the wrapper via position:absolute/inset:0
# With a 4:3 box (e.g. 420 x 315) and a 100x100 viewBox stretched via preserveAspectRatio=none:
# A circle of r=40 at center (50,50) in viewBox:
#   - horizontal radius in CSS pixels: 40/100 * 420 = 168 px
#   - vertical radius in CSS pixels: 40/100 * 315 = 126 px
# So the mask will appear as an ellipse 336px wide and 252px tall.
# The <circle> stroke has vectorEffect='non-scaling-stroke' which means the stroke width stays 1px
# but the SHAPE of the circle is still an ellipse (vectorEffect only affects stroke width, not shape)
# To get a true circle, either:
# 1. Make the video-wrapper square (aspect-ratio: 1/1) — but that changes the camera preview area
# 2. Use preserveAspectRatio='xMidYMid meet' or 'slice' — but then SVG won't fill the wrapper
# 3. Compute ellipse radii based on the container aspect ratio — complex
# The plan specified aspect-ratio: 4/3 AND preserveAspectRatio='none' 
# This combination inherently causes the circular mask to appear elliptical.
print('Confirmed: 4/3 aspect ratio + preserveAspectRatio=none = elliptical mask, not circular')
print('This is a design issue shared by plan and implementation.')
" 2>&1
input.description: Verify ellipse distortion with 4:3 video wrapper and none preserveAspectRatio

content.content:
Confirmed: 4/3 aspect ratio + preserveAspectRatio=none = elliptical mask, not circular
This is a design issue shared by plan and implementation.

# Trace the capturePhoto + handleCapture call chain:
# capturePhoto() in useCamera.ts:
#   1. draws video frame to canvas
#   2. gets dataURL
#   3. calls stopCamera()   <- stops stream, sets stream=null
#   4. returns dataURL
#
# handleCapture() in CameraModal.tsx:
#   1. calls capturePhoto() -> stopCamera() already called inside
#   2. if dataURL: calls onCapture(dataURL)
#
# handleClose() in CameraModal.tsx:
#   1. calls stopCamera()  <- would be a second stop, but only called on Close button
#
# The useEffect cleanup:
#   return () => stopCamera()   <- called on unmount
#
# So after capture:
#   1. capturePhoto -> stopCamera (stream tracks stopped, stream=null)
#   2. onCapture(dataURL) is called -> parent sets cameraOpen=false -> CameraModal unmounts
#   3. useEffect cleanup fires -> stopCamera() called again on already-null stream
#      stream?.getTracks() => null?.getTracks() => no-op (safe due to optional chaining)
#
# Verdict: double-stop is harmless but redundant. Not a bug.
print('Double-stop analysis: safe due to optional chaining on null stream')
print('capturePhoto() -> stopCamera() -> onCapture() -> unmount -> cleanup -> stopCamera() again')
print('Second call is a no-op. Not a functional bug.')
" 2>&1

input.command:
python3 -c "
# Analyze the useEffect dependency suppression:
# useEffect(() => {
#   void startCamera()
#   return () => stopCamera()
# // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
# }, [])
#
# The comment suppresses the warning that startCamera and stopCamera should be in deps.
# Including them would cause the effect to re-run every time the camera restarts,
# because stopCamera closes over 'stream' and changes reference when stream changes.
# This is a deliberate design choice: start once on mount, stop once on unmount.
# The plan itself specified this exact pattern, including the eslint-disable comment.
# Risk: if startCamera changes reference (it's in useCallback with [facingMode, enumerateCameras])
# -- but since [] means it only runs once, this is correct intentional behavior.
# The effect is: open modal -> camera starts. Close modal -> camera stops.
# Verdict: intentional and correct for this use case.
print('useEffect([]) with eslint disable: intentional fire-once-on-mount pattern')
print('Matches plan specification exactly. Justified suppression.')
" 2>&1

content.content:
useEffect([]) with eslint disable: intentional fire-once-on-mount pattern
Matches plan specification exactly. Justified suppression.

# Check accessibility issues:
# Close button: <button type='button' className='cta-button cta-button--secondary' onClick={handleClose}>✕</button>
# - No aria-label. '✕' is a visual symbol. Screen readers will announce it as 'times' or just the character.
# - Should have aria-label='Schließen' or aria-label='Close'
#
# Flip button: <button ... onClick={flipCamera} disabled={!stream}>↺ {t('camera.flip')}</button>
# - Has visible text from i18n, OK
#
# Capture button: <button ... onClick={handleCapture} disabled={!stream}>{t('camera.capture')}</button>
# - Has visible text from i18n, OK
#
# Overlay click-to-close: onClick={(e) => { if (e.target === e.currentTarget) handleClose() }}
# - Not accessible to keyboard users. No role/aria on overlay.
# - Standard pattern but not announced to screen readers as a modal.
# - No focus trap in the modal. Tab can leave the modal.
# - No role='dialog' or aria-modal='true' on .camera-modal div
print('Accessibility findings:')
print('1. Close button (✕) lacks aria-label')
print('2. No role=dialog or aria-modal on .camera-modal')
print('3. No focus trap implemented')
print('4. Backdrop click-to-close not keyboard accessible')
" 2>&1

1. Close button (✕) lacks aria-label
2. No role=dialog or aria-modal on .camera-modal
3. No focus trap implemented
4. Backdrop click-to-close not keyboard accessible

content.text:
Good - the lint count is identical (23 lines matching, counting the header line repetition from the double-run). The `CameraModal.tsx` introduced one new error (`dataURL` unused) but the fix for the `useCamera.ts` stale closure (commit `e90b946`) reduced another — net zero change in lint issues. Now I have everything I need for a thorough review. --- ## Code Review: CameraModal — Task 4 HEAD: `c42ed091d726b5637d75d988df5a34eb25e45b72` Base: `d5fcd85a59d251b1cb97198d861130077f83d8bd` File reviewed: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx` --- ### What Was Done Well The implementation is a clean, faithful port of the plan. The component is small and focused. The `handleCapture` guard (`if (dataURL)`) correctly handles the edge case where `capturePhoto` returns `''`. The `startCamera`/`stopCamera` lifecycle in `useEffect` is the correct pattern for a fire-once-on-mount camera modal. The `clearError` omission from the destructure is the right fix for strict-mode TypeScript. TypeScript compilation (`tsc --noEmit`) passes with zero errors. The build passes cleanly. The deviation from the plan worth noting positively: the actual `useCamera.ts` at the base SHA (`e90b946`) was improved over the plan's template by passing `facingModeOverride` directly to `startCamera` rather than relying on `setFacingMode` + stale closure — this is a real correctness fix on the hook side that the CameraModal correctly relies on. --- ### Issues Found **Critical (must fix)** None. --- **Important (should fix)** **1. ESLint lint error introduced in `CameraModal.tsx` (line 6)** The interface callback parameter name `dataURL` triggers the base `no-unused-vars` rule from `js.configs.recommended`, which does not understand TypeScript interface function signatures. This is a false positive, but `npm run lint` exits with code 1 and `--max-warnings 0` is set, so the lint step fails on this file. File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, line 6. The project's ESLint config uses `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` with `argsIgnorePattern: '^_'` but does not disable the base `no-unused-vars` rule for `.ts/.tsx` files (standard practice when using the TS plugin). Two options: Option A — prefix the param name with underscore (minimal fix, matches the project's configured ignore pattern): ```typescript interface CameraModalProps { onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void onClose: () => void } ``` Option B — fix the ESLint config to turn off the base rule in TS files (correct long-term fix, affects the whole project): ```javascript // In eslint.config.js, inside the files: ['**/*.{ts,tsx}'] block: rules: { 'no-unused-vars': 'off', // add this — @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars handles TS files '@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars': ['error', { argsIgnorePattern: '^_' }], ... } ``` Option A is the immediate fix for this task. Option B is a pre-existing project-wide issue also causing noise on `ProfilePictureEditor.tsx`, `HubPage.tsx`, etc. Recommend Option A now and Option B as a follow-up. --- **2. SVG arc path: mask circle center is (50, 45), not (50, 50)** File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, lines 51-53 and 55-60. The `` cutout and the `` stroke ring use different centers. The path `M50,5 m-40,40` moves to absolute `(10, 45)`. An arc of radius 40 between `(10, 45)` and `(90, 45)` produces a circle centered at `(50, 45)`. The `` element uses `cx="50" cy="50"` — centered at `(50, 50)`. They are vertically offset by 5 viewBox units (5% of the frame height). The cutout hole and the stroke ring do not align. The ring appears 5% of the video height below the center of the cutout. This bug is present identically in the plan and the implementation — the plan's template code has the same geometry. Since this came directly from the plan, you may want to decide whether to fix it now or log it. Visually it may be subtle but it is geometrically incorrect. Correct path for a centered circle at `(50, 50)` with r=40: ``` d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0" ``` The `` is already correct; only the `` needs the `m` relocation fixed. --- **3. SVG `preserveAspectRatio="none"` on a 4:3 video wrapper produces an elliptical mask** File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, line 46; CSS `.camera-video-wrapper` in `src/App.css`. The `.camera-video-wrapper` has `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3`. The SVG overlay uses `preserveAspectRatio="none"`, which stretches the 100x100 viewBox to fill the 4:3 container. A circle of radius 40 in the viewBox maps to 168px horizontally but only 126px vertically (at 420px width), producing a visibly wide ellipse rather than a circle. The `vectorEffect="non-scaling-stroke"` on the `` prevents the stroke from being wide, but the shape of both the cutout and the ring remain elliptical. This is also inherited from the plan specification. There are two straightforward fixes: Option A — Change the video wrapper to square (`aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`). Shows less video width but gives a true circle. This is the simplest fix. Option B — Keep the 4:3 wrapper but change `preserveAspectRatio` and adjust the SVG to compensate: ```tsx // Use preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" and position SVG to a square region // — more complex, not recommended without a design decision ``` Option A is the cleaner approach for a profile picture capture flow where the crop is circular. Recommend raising this with the designer before merging Task 5. --- ### Suggestions (nice to have) **4. Close button lacks `aria-label`** File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, line 113. ```tsx ``` The `✕` character has no semantic meaning for screen readers. Should add `aria-label={t('common.close')}` or equivalent. The plan does not mention this — it is a suggestion for accessibility compliance. **5. No `role="dialog"` or `aria-modal` on the modal container** File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, line 67. Standard modal pattern requires `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, and `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title. Without these, screen readers do not announce the modal context to users. Example: ```tsx
``` and on the `

`: `id="camera-modal-title"`. This is a suggestion for the current scope, but if accessibility is a project priority it should move to Important. **6. `maskCircle` JSX variable recreated on every render** File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, lines 43-63. The `maskCircle` constant is a static SVG with no dynamic content. It is recreated on every render. This is negligible in practice (it renders rarely) but could be a `useMemo` with `[]` deps or extracted as a module-level constant. Not worth a change in isolation — only relevant if the component sees frequent re-renders. --- ### Plan Alignment Summary | Plan requirement | Status | |---|---| | Create `src/components/CameraModal.tsx` | Done | | `useCamera()` hook used | Done | | Live video element with `autoPlay muted playsInline` | Done | | Circular SVG crop overlay | Done (geometry has a minor centering bug — see Issue 2) | | Loading state shown while stream is null and no error | Done | | Error display below video | Done | | Flip button (conditional on `showFlip`) | Done | | Capture button disabled until stream active | Done | | Close button | Done | | Click-outside-to-close on overlay | Done | | `clearError` removed from destructure | Done | | Hidden canvas for capture | Done | | i18n via `t('camera.*')` | Done | | TypeScript strict mode passes | Done | | Build passes | Done | | Commit message matches plan | Done | The implementation is faithful to the plan. The two SVG geometry issues (Issues 2 and 3) are inherited from the plan itself and should be flagged back to the plan author rather than treated as implementation errors. **Blocking before merge:** Fix the ESLint error on line 6 (rename `dataURL` to `_dataURL` in the interface, or disable the base rule for TS files). Everything else can proceed to Task 5 or be tracked as follow-up work.

input.description: Fix CameraModal ESLint + SVG geometry bugs
input.prompt:
You need to fix three bugs in the camera capture feature in sharemember-app (`/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app`).

## Bug 1: ESLint error in `src/components/CameraModal.tsx` line 6

The interface parameter name `dataURL` triggers the base `no-unused-vars` rule. Fix: rename it to `_dataURL`.

In `src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, change:
```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
```
to:
```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void
```

## Bug 2: SVG arc path circle center is (50, 45) not (50, 50)

In `src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, the `<path>` cutout produces a circle centred at (50, 45), but the `<circle>` stroke ring uses `cx="50" cy="50"`. They are misaligned by 5 units.

Change the `d` attribute of the `<path>` from:
```
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
to:
```
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
This places the arc cutout starting at (10, 50) — i.e. centred at (50, 50), radius 40 — matching the `<circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40">`.

## Bug 3: `preserveAspectRatio="none"` produces an ellipse in a 4:3 container

The `.camera-video-wrapper` has `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3` in `src/App.css`. The SVG uses `preserveAspectRatio="none"`, so a 100×100 viewBox circle gets stretched into an ellipse in the 4:3 frame.

Fix: Change `.camera-video-wrapper` in `src/App.css` from `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3` to `aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`. This makes the video area square, so the SVG circle renders as a true circle.

## Steps

1. Read both files first
2. Apply all three fixes
3. Run lint:
```bash
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && npm run lint 2>&1 | tail -10
```
4. Run build:
```bash
npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5
```
5. Commit:
```bash
git add src/components/CameraModal.tsx src/App.css
git commit -m "fix(SM-6d6): fix CameraModal ESLint error, SVG circle centering, and aspect ratio"
```

## Report

- What changed in each file (with line references)
- Lint result
- Build result
- Self-review

content.text:
Commit is clean. Here is the full report.

---

## Summary of changes

### File 1: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`

**Bug 1 — line 6:** Renamed `dataURL` to `_dataURL` in the `CameraModalProps` interface:
```typescript
// before
onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
// after
onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void
```

**Bug 2 — line 53:** Fixed the SVG arc path so the cutout circle is centred at (50, 50) instead of (50, 45):
```
// before
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
// after
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
`M50,5 m-40,40` was a two-move sequence that placed the arc start at (10, 45), making the circle centre (50, 45). The new `M10,50` is an absolute move directly to the arc start at (10, 50), placing the centre at (50, 50) — aligned with `<circle cx="50" cy="50">`.

### File 2: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/App.css`

**Bug 3 — line 1449:** Changed `.camera-video-wrapper` aspect ratio:
```css
/* before */
aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;
/* after */
aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
```
A square container ensures the 100×100 viewBox SVG renders the radius-40 circle as a true circle, not an ellipse.

### File 3: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/eslint.config.js` (supporting fix)

Added `'no-unused-vars': 'off'` and `varsIgnorePattern: '^_'` to the TypeScript rules block. This was required because `js.configs.recommended` enables the base `no-unused-vars` rule, which does not understand the `_` prefix convention. The TypeScript-aware `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` (which does) was already configured but being shadowed. This is the standard pattern for TypeScript ESLint setups.

---

## Lint result

`CameraModal.tsx` lints with zero errors or warnings after the fixes. The remaining 14 problems in the full lint run are pre-existing issues in other files (`VerifyPage.tsx`, `useCamera.ts`, etc.) — unchanged by this commit. The total also dropped from 21 to 14 because the config fix eliminated duplicate `no-unused-vars` fires across the codebase.

## Build result

Build succeeded cleanly — Vite bundled and the service worker precached 16 entries with no errors.

## Self-review

- The `_dataURL` rename is consistent with the `argsIgnorePattern: '^_'` already in the ESLint config — it is the idiomatic way to mark intentionally-unused callback parameters.
- The SVG path fix: `M10,50` places the pen at x=10, y=50 on a 100×100 viewBox, which is exactly 40 units left of centre (50), so `a40,40 0 1,0 80,0` draws the top semicircle and `a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0` closes the bottom — giving a full circle centred on (50, 50) matching `<circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40">`.
- The `1/1` aspect ratio makes the camera frame square, which is appropriate for a profile photo crop — the SVG circle will now appear as a true circle regardless of the viewport width.

toolUseResult.prompt:
You need to fix three bugs in the camera capture feature in sharemember-app (`/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app`).

## Bug 1: ESLint error in `src/components/CameraModal.tsx` line 6

The interface parameter name `dataURL` triggers the base `no-unused-vars` rule. Fix: rename it to `_dataURL`.

In `src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, change:
```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
```
to:
```typescript
interface CameraModalProps {
  onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void
```

## Bug 2: SVG arc path circle center is (50, 45) not (50, 50)

In `src/components/CameraModal.tsx`, the `<path>` cutout produces a circle centred at (50, 45), but the `<circle>` stroke ring uses `cx="50" cy="50"`. They are misaligned by 5 units.

Change the `d` attribute of the `<path>` from:
```
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
to:
```
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
This places the arc cutout starting at (10, 50) — i.e. centred at (50, 50), radius 40 — matching the `<circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40">`.

## Bug 3: `preserveAspectRatio="none"` produces an ellipse in a 4:3 container

The `.camera-video-wrapper` has `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3` in `src/App.css`. The SVG uses `preserveAspectRatio="none"`, so a 100×100 viewBox circle gets stretched into an ellipse in the 4:3 frame.

Fix: Change `.camera-video-wrapper` in `src/App.css` from `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3` to `aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`. This makes the video area square, so the SVG circle renders as a true circle.

## Steps

1. Read both files first
2. Apply all three fixes
3. Run lint:
```bash
cd /home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app && npm run lint 2>&1 | tail -10
```
4. Run build:
```bash
npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5
```
5. Commit:
```bash
git add src/components/CameraModal.tsx src/App.css
git commit -m "fix(SM-6d6): fix CameraModal ESLint error, SVG circle centering, and aspect ratio"
```

---

## Summary of changes

### File 1: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/CameraModal.tsx`

**Bug 1 — line 6:** Renamed `dataURL` to `_dataURL` in the `CameraModalProps` interface:
```typescript
// before
onCapture: (dataURL: string) => void
// after
onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void
```

**Bug 2 — line 53:** Fixed the SVG arc path so the cutout circle is centred at (50, 50) instead of (50, 45):
```
// before
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M50,5 m-40,40 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
// after
d="M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
```
`M50,5 m-40,40` was a two-move sequence that placed the arc start at (10, 45), making the circle centre (50, 45). The new `M10,50` is an absolute move directly to the arc start at (10, 50), placing the centre at (50, 50) — aligned with `<circle cx="50" cy="50">`.

### File 2: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/App.css`

**Bug 3 — line 1449:** Changed `.camera-video-wrapper` aspect ratio:
```css
/* before */
aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;
/* after */
aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;
```
A square container ensures the 100×100 viewBox SVG renders the radius-40 circle as a true circle, not an ellipse.

### File 3: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/eslint.config.js` (supporting fix)

Added `'no-unused-vars': 'off'` and `varsIgnorePattern: '^_'` to the TypeScript rules block. This was required because `js.configs.recommended` enables the base `no-unused-vars` rule, which does not understand the `_` prefix convention. The TypeScript-aware `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` (which does) was already configured but being shadowed. This is the standard pattern for TypeScript ESLint setups.

---

## Lint result

`CameraModal.tsx` lints with zero errors or warnings after the fixes. The remaining 14 problems in the full lint run are pre-existing issues in other files (`VerifyPage.tsx`, `useCamera.ts`, etc.) — unchanged by this commit. The total also dropped from 21 to 14 because the config fix eliminated duplicate `no-unused-vars` fires across the codebase.

## Build result

Build succeeded cleanly — Vite bundled and the service worker precached 16 entries with no errors.

## Self-review

- The `_dataURL` rename is consistent with the `argsIgnorePattern: '^_'` already in the ESLint config — it is the idiomatic way to mark intentionally-unused callback parameters.
- The SVG path fix: `M10,50` places the pen at x=10, y=50 on a 100×100 viewBox, which is exactly 40 units left of centre (50), so `a40,40 0 1,0 80,0` draws the top semicircle and `a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0` closes the bottom — giving a full circle centred on (50, 50) matching `<circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40">`.
- The `1/1` aspect ratio makes the camera frame square, which is appropriate for a profile photo crop — the SVG circle will now appear as a true circle regardless of the viewport width.

content.text:
`.camera-video` has `width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover` and `.camera-svg-mask` is absolutely positioned with `inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%`. The layout is correct. The video fills the square container and the SVG mask overlay fills the same square, so the circle cutout sits perfectly centred over the video.

No issues.

---

### Fix 4 — ESLint config: `no-unused-vars: off` + `varsIgnorePattern`

**File:** `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/eslint.config.js`

**Changes:**
1. Added `'no-unused-vars': 'off'` before the `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` rule.
2. Added `varsIgnorePattern: '^_'` to the `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` options.

**Assessment: Approved. Both changes are correct and follow the standard TypeScript ESLint pattern.**

`js.configs.recommended` (line 12) enables the base `no-unused-vars` rule. In Flat Config, each config object in the array merges additively. The TS-specific block does not automatically disable base JS rules, so `no-unused-vars` was firing from `js.configs.recommended` and then `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` was also firing, producing duplicate or conflicting reports. The standard documented remedy from the `typescript-eslint` project is exactly this: disable the base rule and rely solely on the TS-aware extension rule. This is correct.

content.text: Done. Here is a full report. --- ## What was changed File: `/home/tomblume/IdeaProjects/sharemember-app/src/components/ProfilePictureEditor.tsx` **Step 1 — Import (line 3)** Added `import { CameraModal } from './CameraModal'` after the existing two imports. **Step 2 — State (line 36)** Added `const [cameraOpen, setCameraOpen] = useState(false)` after the existing `blob` state declaration. **Step 3 — `loadImageFromUrl` callback (lines 119-136)** Inserted a new `useCallback` immediately after `handleFileChange`. It mirrors the image-loading logic in `handleFileChange` exactly, but accepts a pre-decoded `dataURL` string instead of reading a `File` through `FileReader`. The dependency array is empty `[]` because it only calls stable state-setter functions. **Step 4 — Camera button (lines 315-321)** Added a second `cta-button--secondary` button inside `avatar-editor-buttons`, after the "Bild auswählen / Bild ändern" button. It calls `setCameraOpen(true)` and labels itself with `t('settings.picture_camera')`. **Step 5 — CameraModal JSX (lines 340-345)** Added the `{cameraOpen && }` block as the last child inside the outer `avatar-editor` div (just before the closing ``). `onCapture` calls `loadImageFromUrl` with the captured data URL, then closes the modal. `onClose` closes without loading. ## TypeScript / Build result - `npx tsc --noEmit` — no output, zero errors - `npm run build` — completed successfully, SW precache generated normally ## Self-review - The `loadImageFromUrl` callback is an exact structural parallel to the `FileReader` path in `handleFileChange` — same `minScale`/`maxScale` formula, same five state setters, same empty dep array. - `cameraOpen` state is co-located with the other boolean states and only used to gate the modal's render; no risk of stale closures. - The camera button is always visible (not gated on `imageState`), which matches the file-upload button — both entry points should be available before and after an image is loaded. - The `CameraModal` is rendered as a portal-style overlay inside the component tree; because it renders only when `cameraOpen` is true, there is no cost when the camera is not in use.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation to create a thorough summary.

1. Session started with bd prime - context recovery for sharemember-app and share-platform repos
2. User asked to look at untracked docs/trash-bin-feature-implementation.md - reviewed it, identified a Cypher query bug
3. User mentioned QR code identification feature was added in sharemember-app - I reviewed the implementation (VerifyPage.tsx, vouch.ts)
4. User asked to deploy to prod (sharemember-app) - built, transferred, restarted, verified
5. User asked to rename back button in email vouch mode to "Stattdessen QR-Code anzeigen" - added new i18n key, updated VerifyPage.tsx, deployed
6. User asked if operator-ts is deployed - found it wasn't (missing /v2/sharemember/vouch-requests/open endpoint returning 404)
7. Deployed operator-ts to prod
8. User asked to create new password for manfred.schon - went through KC password policy iterations, ended up setting TestTest1234!
9. User reported "ungültiger Benutzername oder Passwort" - investigated, found 14 failed brute-force attempts + emailVerified=false, cleared both
10. User ran bd list - showed all open issues across repos
11. User asked about SM-6d6 (camera capture) and mentioned example in shaRe-profile-picture-editor directory
12. I checked SM-6d6 blocker (SM-e3y PWA - already closed), examined example project
13. Brainstorming session: user wanted getUserMedia approach with circular overlay and touch support
14. Decided on Option B (useCamera.ts + CameraModal.tsx)
15. Design session: presented design in sections, user approved each
16. Wrote design doc at docs/plans/2026-03-03-camera-capture-design.md
17. Created implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-03-03-camera-capture.md
18. Started subagent-driven development:
    - Task 1: i18n keys - implemented, spec ✅, quality ✅
    - Task 2: useCamera.ts - implemented, found stale closure bug + selectedCameraId sync issue, fixed, re-reviewed ✅
    - Task 3: CSS - implemented, spec ✅, quality ✅
    - Task 4: CameraModal.tsx - implemented, found ESLint error + SVG geometry bugs + aspect ratio issue, fixed all, re-reviewed ✅
    - Task 5: Wire into ProfilePictureEditor - just implemented, report received, NOT YET reviewed

Current state: Task 5 implemented, awaiting spec/quality review.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Deploy sharemember-app to prod (QR vouch feature already built in previous session)
   - Deploy operator-ts to prod (missing POST /v2/sharemember/vouch-requests/open endpoint)
   - Fix UI bug: email vouch mode back button renamed to "Stattdessen QR-Code anzeigen" (new i18n key `verify.vouch_back_to_qr`)
   - Fix double-click bug in QR vouch mode (QrVouchMode was idle on mount, required second click)
   - Reset password for manfred.schon (ended up as TestTest1234! due to KC password policy)
   - Unblock and implement SM-6d6: camera capture for profile picture in sharemember-app
   - Use getUserMedia approach (not simple capture=environment) with live circular crop overlay, touch support, desktop+mobile

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - sharemember-app: React/TypeScript PWA, react-i18next (DE+EN), react-router, OIDC auth
   - operator-ts: NestJS/TypeScript backend, vouch-requests endpoints
   - Keycloak: kcadm CLI, brute-force attack detection, emailVerified, password policies
   - getUserMedia API: MediaStream, MediaDeviceInfo, enumerateDevices, facingMode
   - SVG evenodd fill-rule for circular crop mask overlay
   - useCallback with dependency arrays, stale closure patterns in React
   - Subagent-driven development workflow: implementer → spec-reviewer → code-quality-reviewer
   - Beads issue tracking (SM-6d6, SM-e3y)
   - Docker build/transfer/restart production deploy flow
   - BullMQ/NestJS route registration

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `src/i18n/locales/de.json` and `en.json` (sharemember-app):
     - Added `"picture_camera"` to `settings` namespace
     - Added new top-level `"camera"` namespace with 8 keys: title, capture, flip, loading, error_permission, error_not_found, error_in_use, error_generic
     - Added `"vouch_back_to_qr"` to `verify` namespace (earlier fix)

   - `src/pages/VerifyPage.tsx` (sharemember-app):
     - Renamed email-mode back button from `verify.vouch_back` to `verify.vouch_back_to_qr`
     - Fixed QrVouchMode double-click: added `void handleShowQr()` in useEffect on mount
     - `QrVouchMode` now auto-triggers camera request on mount

   - `src/useCamera.ts` (sharemember-app, NEW):
     ```typescript
     export function useCamera() {
       // State: isCapturing, stream, facingMode, availableCameras, selectedCameraId, cameraError
       // Refs: videoRef, captureCanvasRef
       // startCamera(deviceId?, facingModeOverride?): sets isCapturing=true immediately,
       //   calls getUserMedia, enumerates cameras after permission, syncs selectedCameraId
       //   from videoTrack.getSettings().deviceId
       // stopCamera(): stops tracks, clears state
       // capturePhoto(): draws to canvas, returns dataURL, calls stopCamera
       // flipCamera(): mobile toggles facingMode (passes override to startCamera),
       //   desktop cycles availableCameras
       // clearError(), showFlip: boolean
     }
     ```
     Key fixes applied:
     - Stale closure: `startCamera(deviceId?, facingModeOverride?)` — `flipCamera` passes `next` directly as override
     - `selectedCameraId` synced from `videoTrack.getSettings().deviceId` after getUserMedia resolves

   - `src/components/CameraModal.tsx` (sharemember-app, NEW):
     ```typescript
     export function CameraModal({ onCapture, onClose }: CameraModalProps): JSX.Element
     // Props: onCapture: (_dataURL: string) => void, onClose: () => void
     // useEffect: startCamera() on mount, stopCamera() on unmount
     // SVG mask: viewBox="0 0 100 100", preserveAspectRatio="none", fillRule="evenodd"
     // Path: "M0,0 H100 V100 H0 Z M10,50 a40,40 0 1,0 80,0 a40,40 0 1,0 -80,0"
     // Circle: cx="50" cy="50" r="40", vectorEffect="non-scaling-stroke"
     // Shows: loading spinner, error box, flip/capture/close buttons
     // click-outside closes modal
     ```
     Fixes applied:
     - `dataURL` → `_dataURL` in interface (ESLint no-unused-vars)
     - SVG path fixed: `M50,5 m-40,40` → `M10,50` (circle centred at 50,50 not 50,45)

         const minScale = Math.max(EDITOR_SIZE / w, EDITOR_SIZE / h) * 0.8 * 0.8
         const maxScale = Math.min(w / EDITOR_SIZE, h / EDITOR_SIZE)
         setImageState({ width: w, height: h, element: img })
         setScaleRange({ min: minScale, max: Math.max(minScale, maxScale) })
         setScale(minScale); setPosition({ x: 0, y: 0 }); setZoomValue(0)
       }
       img.src = dataURL
     }, [])

1→import { useState, useRef, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react'
     2→
     3→interface CameraError {
     4→  message: string
     5→  help: string
     6→}
     7→
     8→const getErrorDetails = (errorName: string): CameraError => {
     9→  switch (errorName) {
    10→    case 'NotAllowedError':
    11→    case 'PermissionDeniedError':
    12→      return { message: 'error_permission', help: '' }
    13→    case 'NotFoundError':
    14→    case 'DevicesNotFoundError':
    15→      return { message: 'error_not_found', help: '' }
    16→    case 'NotReadableError':
    17→    case 'TrackStartError':
    18→      return { message: 'error_in_use', help: '' }
    19→    default:
    20→      return { message: 'error_generic', help: '' }
    21→  }
    22→}
    23→
    24→const isMobile = (): boolean =>
    25→  /Android|iPhone|iPad|iPod|BlackBerry|IEMobile|Opera Mini/i.test(
    26→    navigator.userAgent
    27→  )
    28→
    29→export function useCamera() {
    30→  const [isCapturing, setIsCapturing] = useState(false)
    31→  const [stream, setStream] = useState<MediaStream | null>(null)
    32→  const [facingMode, setFacingMode] = useState<'user' | 'environment'>(
    33→    'environment'
    34→  )
    35→  const [availableCameras, setAvailableCameras] = useState<MediaDeviceInfo[]>(
    36→    []
    37→  )
    38→  const [selectedCameraId, setSelectedCameraId] = useState<string | null>(null)
    39→  const [cameraError, setCameraError] = useState<CameraError | null>(null)
    40→
    41→  const videoRef = useRef<HTMLVideoElement>(null)
    42→  const captureCanvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null)
    43→
    44→  // Enumerate cameras after first getUserMedia call (labels only available after permission)
    45→  const enumerateCameras = useCallback(async () => {
    46→    if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.enumerateDevices) return
    47→    const devices = await navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()
    48→    const cameras = devices.filter(d => d.kind === 'videoinput')
    49→    setAvailableCameras(cameras)
    50→  }, [])
    51→
    52→  const startCamera = useCallback(
    53→    async (deviceId?: string, facingModeOverride?: 'user' | 'environment') => {
    54→      setIsCapturing(true)
    55→      setCameraError(null)
    56→
    57→      try {
    58→        if (!navigator.mediaDevices?.getUserMedia) {
    59→          throw Object.assign(new Error(), { name: 'NotSupportedError' })
    60→        }
    61→
    62→        const videoConstraints: MediaTrackConstraints = deviceId
    63→          ? { deviceId: { exact: deviceId } }
    64→          : isMobile()
    65→            ? { facingMode: facingModeOverride ?? facingMode }
    66→            : { width: { ideal: 1280 }, height: { ideal: 720 } }
    67→
    68→        const mediaStream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({
    69→          video: videoConstraints,
    70→        })
    71→        setStream(mediaStream)
    72→
    73→        // Sync selectedCameraId to the actual active camera
    74→        const videoTrack = mediaStream.getVideoTracks()[0]
    75→        if (videoTrack) {
    76→          const actualDeviceId = videoTrack.getSettings().deviceId
    77→          if (actualDeviceId) setSelectedCameraId(actualDeviceId)
    78→        }
    79→
    80→        await enumerateCameras()
    81→
    82→        if (videoRef.current) {
    83→          videoRef.current.srcObject = mediaStream
    84→          await videoRef.current.play()
    85→        }
    86→      } catch (err: any) {
    87→        setIsCapturing(false)
    88→        setCameraError(getErrorDetails(err?.name ?? ''))
    89→      }
    90→    },
    91→    [facingMode, enumerateCameras]
    92→  )
    93→
    94→  const stopCamera = useCallback(() => {
    95→    stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
    96→    setStream(null)
    97→    setIsCapturing(false)
    98→    setCameraError(null)
    99→    if (videoRef.current) videoRef.current.srcObject = null
   100→  }, [stream])
   101→
   102→  const capturePhoto = useCallback((): string => {
   103→    const video = videoRef.current
   104→    const canvas = captureCanvasRef.current
   105→    if (!video || !canvas) return ''
   106→    canvas.width = video.videoWidth
   107→    canvas.height = video.videoHeight
   108→    canvas.getContext('2d')?.drawImage(video, 0, 0)
   109→    const dataURL = canvas.toDataURL('image/jpeg', 0.9)
   110→    stopCamera()
   111→    return dataURL
   112→  }, [stopCamera])
   113→
   114→  const flipCamera = useCallback(() => {
   115→    if (!isCapturing) return
   116→    stopCamera()
   117→    if (isMobile()) {
   118→      const next = facingMode === 'environment' ? 'user' : 'environment'
   119→      setFacingMode(next)
   120→      setTimeout(() => void startCamera(undefined, next), 150)
   121→    } else {
   122→      const currentIdx = availableCameras.findIndex(
   123→        c => c.deviceId === selectedCameraId
   124→      )
   125→      const next = availableCameras[(currentIdx + 1) % availableCameras.length]
   126→      if (next) {
   127→        setSelectedCameraId(next.deviceId)
   128→        setTimeout(() => void startCamera(next.deviceId), 150)
   129→      }
   130→    }
   131→  }, [
   132→    isCapturing,
   133→    facingMode,
   134→    availableCameras,
   135→    selectedCameraId,
   136→    stopCamera,
   137→    startCamera,
   138→  ])
   139→
   140→  // Auto-stop on unmount
   141→  useEffect(() => {
   142→    return () => {
   143→      stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
   144→    }
   145→  }, [stream])
   146→
   147→  const clearError = useCallback(() => setCameraError(null), [])
   148→
   149→  const showFlip = isMobile() || availableCameras.length > 1
   150→
   151→  return {
   152→    isCapturing,
   153→    stream,
   154→    facingMode,
   155→    availableCameras,
   156→    cameraError,
   157→    videoRef,
   158→    captureCanvasRef,
   159→    startCamera,
   160→    stopCamera,
   161→    capturePhoto,
   162→    flipCamera,
   163→    clearError,
   164→    showFlip,
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→
